title: How Much You Should Have Saved in Your 401k By Age? (Updated 2026)
summary: Nick Davis explains that standard 401k benchmarks (based on average/median balances) are misleading because they measure what most people have, not what they need for retirement control. The actual targets should emphasize account diversification (traditional, Roth, taxable) to minimize tax liability and RMD impact, not just balance size. Includes age-specific targets from 35-63 showing required savings multiples and Roth allocation percentages.

title: Why People Get Richer After Retirement (The Hidden Math Most Retirees Miss)
summary: Nick Davis explains the mathematical reasons why many retirees grow wealthier after retirement, counter to conventional expectations. The hidden math involves the spending gap (withdrawal rates below portfolio capacity), tax optimization (effective rates declining from 22-32% to 8-15%), declining spending patterns over time, Social Security COLA adjustments outpacing lifestyle costs, and structured Roth conversions that enable tax-free compounding at rates exceeding pre-retirement returns.

title: Stop Obsessing Over the Wrong Risks
summary: Discusses how investors obsess over temporary market noise (political drama, short-term volatility, elections) rather than permanent risks that actually threaten retirement plans. Identifies actionable risks within investor control: over-concentration, tax anchoring, panic selling, FOMO, leverage, and being too conservative. Emphasizes the importance of systematic approaches and behavioral discipline over trying to beat markets.

title: How Retirees Turn $500K Into Over $2M (The Secret Formula)
summary: Nick Davis explains how retirees can turn $500,000 into over $2 million by understanding five structural features of retirement: leveraging Social Security to reduce portfolio withdrawal pressure, naturally declining spending with age creating larger gaps between portfolio earnings and withdrawals, tax-free Roth account compounding, Social Security cost-of-living adjustments replacing portfolio income needs, and the resulting accelerating compounding effect over the retirement period.

title: If You’ve Saved $2M, Here’s Your Retirement Reality
summary: Nick Davis of Brindle and Bay walks through the full financial reality of retiring with $2 million, covering income capacity using the 4% rule, the cascading tax effects of RMDs and Social Security taxation, IRMAA surcharges, the conversion window opportunity, and the critical survivor scenario where the surviving spouse faces compressed single-filer brackets on the same RMD income.

title: 7 Levels of Wealth in Retirement in 2026: Where are you?
summary: Comprehensive guide to seven distinct wealth levels in retirement ($300K to $7.5M+), explaining how tax implications and optimization strategies differ significantly at each level and why a one-size-fits-all approach fails.

title: Rule of 55 : Are You Retiring Before You're Ready
summary: Financial advisors Nick Davis, Connie, and Andrew from Brindle and Bay discuss the Rule of 55, which allows penalty-free 401k withdrawals for employees who separate from their most recent employer at age 55 or older. The episode emphasizes that having access to funds does not mean one is financially ready to retire, and presents three self-assessment questions for pre-retirees to test true readiness.

title: If You Have a Pension & Social Security, Watch This Before You Claim
summary: Nick Davis explains how the 2025 Social Security Fairness Act repealed the Windfall Elimination Provision and Government Pension Offset, significantly changing retirement planning for public sector workers. He outlines pension-Social Security coordination strategies: pension election types, survivor benefit protection, tax stacking implications, and withdrawal sequencing using guardrails or spending smile approaches.

title: $1.5 Million Isn’t a Magic Number… It’s Now the Bare MINIMUM for Retirement
summary: Nick Davis, CFP, argues that $1.5 million—long considered a comfortable retirement target—now represents the bare minimum rather than a secure goal, driven by cumulative inflation since 2020, longer life expectancies, rising healthcare costs, and tax complexity. He presents data from Northwestern Mutual and Fidelity to quantify how each factor erodes purchasing power, and outlines components of a stronger retirement plan.

title: 7 Things Wealthy Retirees STOPPED Buying to Stay Rich
summary: Nick Davis, CFP, outlines seven spending habits that wealthy retirees tend to abandon in retirement, including frequent new car purchases, unused subscriptions, peer-driven lifestyle upgrades, high-fee investment products, status-signaling spending, impulse buying, and the mindset that retirement requires constant consumption.

title: FPA #nexgen Advisors | Dr. Preston Cherry & Colin Overweg | Having Great Conversations with Clients!
summary: Dr. Preston Cherry joins Colin Overweg for an FPA NexGen Michigan session on how financial advisers can have better client conversations. The discussion covers professional designations for new advisers, the importance of asking open-ended discovery questions, connecting clients' past financial experiences to their present situation and future aspirations, and integrating financial psychology and behavioral skills into the planning process.

title: Non-Qualified Stock Options PART 2 | Taxes | Risk | Strategy | Colin Overweg, CFP®
summary: Colin Overweg analyzes non-qualified stock options (NSOs) exercise strategy, focusing on tax implications and investment portfolio risk. He walks through tax planning using software, addresses bracket management, and discusses state tax complications when relocating from high-tax to zero-tax states. Key strategy: frame NSO exercise decisions as whether you would independently purchase the company stock with that amount of cash.

title: Dividend Stocks For Passive Income?! #retirement #passiveincome
summary: Colin Overweg argues that focusing on dividend-paying stocks for passive income is suboptimal compared to total-return investing. He explains that high-quality companies like Apple, Google, Microsoft, and Berkshire Hathaway pay little or no dividends because they reinvest profits for growth. Overweg demonstrates that a 1% dividend from an S&P 500 index plus 4% portfolio withdrawal equals the same income as 5% dividend stocks, but with significantly higher total returns over time.

title: Taylor Schulte & Colin Overweg | FPA #nexgen Community | Investing, Careers, Marketing and more!
summary: Taylor Schulte, CFP and founder of Define Financial, joins Colin Overweg's FPA NextGen Michigan community to discuss career paths in financial planning, niching down, business ownership vs. employee roles, and marketing strategies for independent advisors.

title: Caleb Silver | FPA #nexgen Guest | Investing, Marketing, Future of the Profession
summary: Caleb Silver, Editor-in-Chief of Investopedia, joins the FPA NextGen Michigan chapter to discuss how financial advisers find information, the importance of content creation as a professional resume, effective use of AI tools, and the value of holistic financial planning beyond portfolio management.

title: RSUs Explained Simply: How Restricted Stock Units are Taxed
summary: Educational explanation of how RSUs (Restricted Stock Units) work and their taxation. Covers the tax treatment upon vesting (taxed as ordinary income at fair market value), post-vesting scenarios including short-term capital gains, and long-term capital gains treatment. Uses a real example of Reddit RSUs to illustrate the concepts.

title: Is It Possible to Invest Too Safely in Retirement? | Avoid This Common Mistake
summary: Financial education addressing the risk of being too conservative in retirement investing. Explains how inflation erodes purchasing power over 20+ year retirements and presents a framework for allocating assets across cash, bonds, and stocks based on time horizons and spending needs. Discusses market return probabilities and the importance of understanding client-specific circumstances in financial planning.

title: Social Security TAXABLE… But pay $0 in TAXES #socialsecurity #taxplanning
summary: Educational explanation of how Social Security can be taxable while resulting in zero tax liability. Demonstrates a real-world tax planning example for a 63-year-old retiree in Florida using annuity withdrawals. Shows how to calculate provisional income, determine Social Security taxability using IRS Publication 915, and how tax credits can eliminate tax liability despite reportable income.

title: Michael Burry's Warning: Is a Dot-Com Crash Coming?
summary: Analysis of Michael Burry's warning about a potential dot-com style market bubble in 2026. Compares current S&P 500 valuations (P/E ratio ~31) to the 1999-2000 dot-com peak (P/E 46), discusses earnings growth fundamentals, market concentration in top 10 companies, and historical returns following previous market peaks. Provides context for both young investors and retirees navigating an expensive market.

title: SpaceX Is Worth $2.4 TRILLION… Would You Buy It?
summary: Analysis of SpaceX's $2.4 trillion valuation following its IPO at $135 per share, examining the company's business segments (rockets, Starlink, xAI) and examining historical patterns of IPO performance to assess investment merit.

title: How To Retire In 5 Years (FULL GUIDE)
summary: Financial planner Eric Amzalag presents four comprehensive strategies for retiring within five years: calculating required portfolio income and withdrawal rates across retirement phases, analyzing Social Security claiming scenarios to optimize guaranteed income, shifting savings to non-retirement accounts for tax efficiency before retirement, and using better portfolio risk measures than traditional asset allocation to manage sequence-of-returns risk.

title: 4 ROTH Retirement Traps That WILL RUIN Your Retirement
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, outlines four Roth conversion traps that can damage retirement outcomes: converting when the cost increases distribution rates to unsustainable levels, paying conversion taxes from pre-tax accounts, failing to calculate a break-even age, and converting when strategic withdrawal sequencing already achieves comparable results without the upfront cost.

title: Can I Realistically Spend $10,000 in Retirement?
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, walks through a case study of a hypothetical couple aged 60 with $1.1 million in assets asking whether they can spend $10,000 per month after taxes in retirement, demonstrating how to identify distribution rate risk and evaluate four dynamic spending strategies including retirement spending smile, floor-and-ceiling, and guardrails.

title: Why Claiming Social Security at 70 Is Worse Than You Think
summary: Analysis of Social Security claiming timing decisions, exposing the limitations of always delaying to age 70. Examines front-loaded distribution rate risk, psychological impacts of unnecessary delayed retirement, time value of money and experiences, break-even analysis, and multi-factor evaluation framework beyond probability metrics.

title: Peak FP Podcast: Retirement Planning Tools Will Ruin Your Retirement
summary: Eric Amzalag argues that probability-of-success retirement planning tools, while useful as a baseline, mislead retirees into delaying retirement and underspending by obscuring three critical deficiencies: overly simplified single-number outputs, no step-by-step action plans, and no contingency frameworks. He recommends supplementing them with dynamic spending tools, distribution rate tables, real-time spending trackers, and portfolio risk metrics.

title: The Retirement Spending Lies All Retirees Believe
summary: Financial planner debunks three major retirement spending myths: that higher early spending reduces retirement success, that 4% rule is mandatory, and that expenses inflate linearly. Proposes distribution rate bands and spending smile patterns as more accurate planning tools.

title: 3 Signs You Have Enough Money To Retire, NOW
summary: Eric Amzalag explains three criteria for assessing retirement readiness: sufficient financial assets, a comprehensive retirement plan with monitoring and contingencies, and meaningful activities beyond work. The video provides a formula-based framework for calculating required portfolio income and discusses the limitations of relying solely on wealth accumulation.

title: Peak FP Podcast: Why Your Retirement Investment’s are At Risk
summary: Podcast episode explaining sequence-of-returns risk from a practical retirement planning perspective. Covers the unequal relationship between market losses and required recoveries, the compounding effect of distributions during downturns, the limitations of Monte Carlo straight-line return assumptions, and a three-part strategy involving comprehension, proper planning, and a bottom-up investment approach using withdrawal rules, asset allocation, and diversification.

title: Why It Makes COMPLETE Sense To Claim Social Security At 62
summary: Argues that claiming Social Security at age 62 can be the optimal strategy for many retirees, using data on actual retirement ages, life expectancy studies, and sequence-of-returns risk scenarios. Presents numerical comparisons showing early claiming reduces early-retirement portfolio distribution rates and portfolio depletion under adverse market conditions.

title: Most Americans Will NEVER Retire: Here's How To Time Yours
summary: Certified financial planner Eric Amzalag explains the 'retirement risk zone' — the 5-10 years before or just after retirement — and outlines two key actions: refining an accumulation plan using both spreadsheet calculators and financial planning software, and stress-testing retirement timing through scenario modeling including forced early retirement.

title: Retirement Withdrawal Strategies Ranked (Best to Worst)
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, ranks four retirement withdrawal strategies—prata (rebalance annually), 4% rule, glide path, and guardrail—across quantitative and qualitative metrics, concluding guardrail strategies best balance peace of mind, execution ease, course-correction opportunities, and spending power.

title: The Saving Paradox: Why Most Retirees Secretly Struggle
summary: Eric Amzalag examines the savings paradox (permission to spend problem), where retirees accumulate wealth but fail to spend it due to psychological fear and loss aversion. The video analyzes research showing affluent retirees rarely drawdown portfolios and discusses how financial industry messaging perpetuates this behavior.

title: I've Built 100 Financial Plans, Here's What Works - Peak FP Podcast
summary: Eric Amzalag presents a prioritized three-phase retirement planning framework: triage (accumulate sufficient assets), stabilization (develop comprehensive income and contingency plans), and optimization (address financial surplus and legacy planning). The framework emphasizes executing a mediocre plan consistently over abandoning complex plans.

title: Retirement Expert Explains: Why Bonds Are Riskier Than Stocks
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, debunks four common retirement investing myths using backtested portfolio data from Portfolio Visualizer: (1) ETFs/mutual funds are always superior to directly held stocks; (2) active strategies are inferior to passive buy-and-hold; (3) bonds are always safe and stocks are always risky; and (4) global diversification reliably hedges US market risk. Backtest results from 2013–2024 and 2000–2024 underpin each argument.

title: Retirement Portfolio Lies: Why Asset Allocation Is A Scam
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP and RICP, argues that standard asset allocation models fail retirement investors by obscuring underlying risks, encouraging impulsive behavior, and creating a confidence-loss spiral. Using the 2022 market downturn as a case study, he advocates replacing simple stock-bond splits with four granular risk measures and a more specific investment approach involving directly held bonds and stocks.

title: A Step-By-Step Guide To Retiring With $650k In Savings
summary: A case study on retirement planning for a 60 and 54-year-old couple with $650k in savings. Demonstrates a five-step financial planning process including assessment, plan construction, implementation, monitoring, and contingency planning. Key strategies include guard rails distribution, Social Security optimization, and portfolio adjustments to improve their retirement probability from 40% to 84% success.

title: How Much Should I Pay My Advisor? 1% vs Fee Only
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, compares four financial advisor fee structures—commission-based, hourly, flat-fee, and assets-under-management (AUM)—explaining the incentive problems with each and arguing that a flat-fee arrangement combined with optional AUM is the most client-aligned model for retirement planning.

title: It's Not Just You: Why Everyone Gave Up Retiring Early ...
summary: CFP Eric Amzalag uses Bureau of Labor Statistics data to challenge the narrative that rising costs make retirement increasingly unachievable. Analyzing 1992-2023 cost of living, income, spending category shares, income quintile effects, and 60/40 portfolio back-tests, he argues that sustainable withdrawal rates are higher than commonly stated and that retirement delays are driven by poor savings discipline rather than economic conditions.

title: Every Broken Retirement Rule Explained in 14 Minutes
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, debunks four common retirement rules of thumb: the 4% rule, the 100-minus-age stock allocation formula, the 25x annual spending savings target, and the assumption that Roth conversions are universally beneficial, arguing that each should be evaluated individually based on a retiree's specific income needs, savings rate, and life expectancy.

title: Watch This Video To Avoid The RMD Tax Time Bomb ...
summary: Educational guide on minimizing taxes from required minimum distributions (RMDs) in retirement. Uses a case study of a retiree to illustrate withdrawal rate analysis, healthcare subsidy optimization, and decision-making between Roth conversions and current-day spending priorities.

title: I Reviewed A Subscribers Retirement Plan (Here’s Where They Went Wrong)
summary: Certified financial planner Eric Amzalag reviews a subscriber's retirement plan for James and Audrey, a couple seeking to retire at 60 with a net worth of $4.3 million. The analysis reveals a 1% probability of success with their current approach, but implementing ten specific recommendations—including more aggressive asset allocation, claiming Social Security at 62, and reducing discretionary gifting—can increase success probability to 63-68%.

title: The Retirement Tax You Shouldn't Pay For But Still Do
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, walks through a client case study of a 59-year-old retiree with $2.5 million who was paralyzed between minimizing income for ACA subsidies versus performing Roth conversions to avoid a future RMD tax bomb. The video applies a guardrails-based withdrawal framework to show the client had far more spending and conversion flexibility than she realized.

title: Retirement Expert Breaks Down How Harris vs Trump Impacts You
summary: Comparative analysis of 2024 presidential candidates' policy proposals affecting retirees across Social Security, taxes, and inflation. Evaluates Trump's proposal to exempt Social Security from income taxes (estimated <1% impact), tax cut permanency, and tariff proposals alongside Harris-Walz premium credit expansion and tax increases on high-income households. Concludes that election outcomes have minimal material impact on most retirees' financial outcomes.

title: Here's The No.1 Sign You Are Ready To Retire NOW (Even If It Doesn't Feel Like It)
summary: Educational guide on identifying retirement readiness through the presence of comprehensive financial systems. Argues that exploring advanced withdrawal and tax strategies is often a subconscious sign of financial preparedness, but true readiness requires understanding mechanics of retirement execution including expense tracking, withdrawal systems, and risk management.

title: I’ve Read 100 Books On Life. Here’s How To Retire Without Regret…
summary: Comprehensive retirement planning guide synthesizing lessons from 100+ books on life and retirement. Presents three-tier retirement hierarchy (predictability, stability, fulfillment) and emphasizes balancing financial planning with happiness, relationships, and health as drivers of meaningful retirement.

title: The 5-Year Rule: Why You Need Less Than You Think To Retire…
summary: Educational guide on the 5-Year Rule for retirement planning, which recommends beginning retirement planning at least 5 years before target retirement date. Explains how retirement timing differs from expectations, the importance of comprehensive planning, and argues financial advisory fees are worth the cost to retire earlier and with confidence.

title: It's A Lie: Why You Won't Run Out Of Money In Retirement
summary: Financial advisor Eric Amzalag addresses misleading headlines about retirement savings, arguing that financial education and prioritization matter more than automated savings alone, using an analogy of boat-building to explain retirement planning fundamentals.

title: It's harsh, but if you want to retire, you need to hear this
summary: Eric Amzalag critiques common retirement planning misconceptions—especially over-reliance on Monte Carlo probability scores and rigid safe withdrawal rate rules—and proposes a dynamic safe withdrawal system using guardrails that adapts to market conditions while maintaining flexibility for retirees.

title: How Much Can I Spend In Retirement EVERY DAY? (By Savings Bracket)
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, demonstrates how much retirees at four different savings brackets ($250K, $500K, $1M, $2M) can spend per day using the modern guardrails withdrawal methodology. The video presents specific initial withdrawal rates, guard rail trigger thresholds, and a practical spreadsheet-based tracking system to manage daily and monthly retirement spending.

title: Retirement Timing Strategies Ranked Worst To Best
summary: CFP Eric Amzalag ranks retirement timing strategies from worst to best: forced retirement, retire-by-feel, portfolio-value-based retirement, and retirement income planning (optimal). He emphasizes that retirement timing should be driven by a structured income plan rather than emotions, arbitrary benchmarks, or external circumstances.

title: Is The 1% Rule Actually Worthwhile in Retirement?
summary: CFP Eric Amzalag critiques the financial services industry's fee structures and argues that retirement planning should be separated from investment management. He advocates for transparent flat or quoted fees for planning services, positioning AUM (assets under management) fees as appropriate only after a competence-based relationship is established through initial planning engagement.

title: Give Me 11 Minutes, And You'll Save 10 Years Of Your Life
summary: CFP Eric Amzalag demonstrates a practical five-step retirement income planning process that enables people to determine if they can retire years earlier than expected. The video provides downloadable spreadsheet templates covering expense estimation, portfolio income requirements, required savings pools, savings timelines, and monitoring systems to optimize retirement readiness.

title: They've Manipulated You: Retirement Is Cheaper Than You Think
summary: Financial planner challenges the common belief that $2.2 million is required for retirement, demonstrating through a case study that most Americans need substantially less due to media fear-mongering, industry incentive structures, and misunderstood financial planning. Illustrates how proper cash flow planning with guardrails enables confident retirement spending decisions.

title: How To Evaluate A Retirement Investment Portfolio
summary: Educational framework for evaluating retirement investment risk by decomposing it into two components: probability of negative outcome and magnitude of consequence. Introduces concentration, exposure, variance, and correlation as practical metrics superior to binary asset allocation thinking, enabling better portfolio management decisions.

title: The 5 Secret Wealth Killers Retirees Don't Notice Until 70
summary: Identifies five critical wealth-draining patterns that trap retirees unaware: herd mentality in financial decisions, investing without cash flow planning, misunderstanding investment risk via binary asset allocation, building retirement plans without professional guidance, and avoiding finances until crisis. Emphasizes proactive planning over reactive problem-solving.

title: Retirement Expert: How I Actually Build A $2M Portfolio
summary: Advanced retirement portfolio construction framework examining how to build a $2 million portfolio using seven key risk management principles: concentration, exposure, variance, market correlation, beta, contingency planning, and rebalancing. Critiques traditional asset allocation approach and advocates for rules-based portfolio management grounded in understanding probability and magnitude of market outcomes, with emphasis on monitoring systems over binary risk classifications.

title: The 4 Stages Of Retirement Planning: Which One Are You In?
summary: Applies competence ladder framework (unconscious incompetence, conscious incompetence, conscious competence, unconscious competence) to retirement planning, explaining psychological stages retirees experience while accumulating expertise. Recommends hiring experienced professionals rather than attempting 10,000-hour mastery solo, emphasizing long-term discipline over complex one-time decisions.

title: Loss Aversion Theory: Why Retirees Are Too Scared To Retire
summary: Explores loss aversion as a psychological barrier to retirement. Shows that 50% portfolio declines are rare (2.6% historically), happen slowly (12+ months), and can be managed through portfolio guardrails rather than avoiding retirement. Challenges media and financial industry incentives to promote fear.

title: Why Retiring At The Same Time As Your Spouse Is A Terrible Idea
summary: Analyzes why staggered spousal retirement outperforms simultaneous retirement. Demonstrates using guardrails that one spouse continuing work reduces withdrawal rates, lowers portfolio strain, preserves healthcare benefits, increases future Social Security benefits, and eases psychological transition from earning to spending.

title: It's Not Too Late To Retire, You're Just Following Outdated Rules
summary: Critiques traditional retirement rules (4% rule, asset allocation, Monte Carlo scores) as fear-based, oversimplified, and serving other interests. Advocates replacing rules with dynamic frameworks like portfolio guardrails that empower retirees to understand and adapt to retirement challenges rather than blindly follow static guidance.

title: Here's the ONE THING All Retirees Wish They Had Done Sooner
summary: Reviews 100 retirement plans to show most retirees wish they had used guardrails-based planning instead of Monte Carlo scores alone. Explains how guardrails translate probability scores into actionable spending adjustment thresholds, providing clarity and confidence for retirement decisions and enabling higher early-life spending.

title: When I Show Retirees This Chart, They Spend 40% More
summary: Financial planner Eric Amzalag demonstrates how a confidence-building chart helped retired clients discover they could spend nearly double their anticipated amount. Using modern guardrails software and realistic withdrawal planning, the video addresses fear of spending in retirement and shows a framework for sustainable income planning beyond the 4% rule.

title: I Was Wrong About Early Retirement (What I Know Now)
summary: Financial advisor Eric Amzalag reconsiders his early retirement philosophy, revealing that numbers alone don't determine retirement success. He emphasizes that non-financial planning—defining meaningful activities, maintaining social connections, and considering identity transitions—is equally critical for genuine retirement satisfaction.

title: If I Wanted To Retire in 2025, Here's The First 3 Numbers I'd Track
summary: Financial planner Eric Amzalag identifies three critical metrics for retirement readiness: required portfolio income (withdrawal rate by phase), personal inflation rate, and guardrails (dynamic spending bands). These metrics replace generic probability-of-success models, providing actionable contingency plans and reducing retirement anxiety.

title: I am 62 with $1.6M. What Role Will My Social Security Income Play?!
summary: A financial planner walks through how he helped a 62- and 59-year-old couple with $1.6M in savings decide when to claim Social Security and retire, using three scenario comparisons—most aggressive, moderate, and least aggressive—built around a guardrails-based withdrawal framework. The case study shows that the couple had more options than they realized and did not need to commit to a fixed claiming strategy to retire with confidence.

title: The Retirement Question Most Retirees Ignore
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, argues that the central question in retirement planning should shift from 'how much money do I need?' to 'how do we prepare for inevitable financial surprises?' Using a client case study of Steve and Amanda who were told to return to work after a 20% market correction, he presents a modern guardrails framework powered by Income Lab software that provides layered contingency plans and spending capacity metrics to help retirees cross the retirement threshold with confidence.

title: If You're Tired of Working But Scared To Retire, Watch This
summary: Financial advisor Eric Amzalag addresses four psychological fears that keep people working despite financial readiness: unknown duration (longevity anxiety), market-timing worry, healthcare cost uncertainty, and identity transition fears. He proposes retirement contingency planning—detailed war-gaming of scenarios—as the antidote to these fears.

title: This Chart Shows When Most Retirements Are At Risk
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, introduces the concept of the retirement risk zone—the transitional period between accumulation and decumulation—and argues it should be reframed as an opportunity zone.

title: You’re Retirement Savings Aren’t The Reason You Haven’t Retired, This Is…
summary: Explores retirement as a transition that replaces financial stressors with new non-financial ones. Discusses how weak financial plans leave retirees paralyzed by uncertainty and unable to enjoy their wealth. Introduces the concept of spending speed limits and guardrails to help retirees feel secure in their withdrawals.

title: What Wealthy 80-Year-Olds Wish They Knew At Retirement
summary: Reveals five consistent regrets wealthy retirees wish they had known earlier: physical capacity decline, lack of legacy clarity, financial passenger vulnerability, dream delay, and permission-to-spend guilt. Argues that wealth cannot compensate for non-financial preparation and introduces a framework to integrate financial and non-financial retirement planning.

title: Its Boring, But This Is The Secret To Being A "Rich Retiree”
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, challenges conventional retirement savings advice that treats retirement accounts as universally superior, arguing that overconcentration in pre-tax accounts causes value confusion and can trap retirees who want to retire early. He explains when Roth versus pre-tax versus non-retirement accounts are optimal using a tax path framework and illustrates with client stories the real cost of ignoring account-type selection.

title: 4 Steps to Managing EXTREME Market Volatility
summary: Addresses how investors should respond to extreme market volatility (triggered by tariff announcements). Critiques reactive decision-making based on news headlines and advocates for a four-step diagnostic approach: check actual portfolio performance, compare to market benchmarks, assess guardrail depth, and verify whether the plan's decision rules have been triggered.

title: I'm 61 With $1.03M, How Much Can I Spend in Retirement?
summary: Case study of a 61-year-old couple with $1.03M in savings evaluating retirement readiness. Contrasts traditional Monte Carlo probability scores with dynamic guardrails planning using Income Labs software, demonstrating how guardrails better capture spending flexibility and real behavioral patterns to determine a safe withdrawal strategy.

title: I Showed This Retiree Where It All Went Wrong...
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, uses a real couple's case to show how a fear-driven annuity purchase in August 2022 reduced their retirement plan's probability of success from 66% to 37% when the husband's life expectancy is modeled realistically.

title: You're In A TRAP: Retirement Sucks If You Wait Too Long
summary: Explores the hidden costs of delaying retirement beyond financial readiness, focusing on the lifespan-health span gap, the opportunity cost of giving peak health years to employers, and practical timelines for transitioning out of work. Argues that working longer than necessary benefits employers and healthcare industries at the expense of the individual.

title: DON’T PANIC (But Check This Rule Before You Retire)
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, introduces the '72-hour recovery rule'—a data-driven framework recommending that investors wait 72 hours before making portfolio adjustments after a single-day market decline of 2% or greater.

title: Why you WON'T Retire - Even Though You Know You COULD Retire...
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, identifies three limiting beliefs that prevent financially ready retirees from actually retiring: over-reliance on Monte Carlo probability-of-success scores, an addiction to accumulating high savings scores, and the pursuit of a perfect retirement plan. He argues that Monte Carlo scores are not actionable plans, that chasing wealth beyond necessity reduces well-being, and that incremental transitions to retirement outperform all-or-nothing approaches.

title: The Shocking Truth About Sudden Retirement!
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, discusses the high likelihood of forced early retirement—citing EBRI data that 40% of Americans retire earlier than planned with the median retiree retiring 3 years ahead of schedule—and explains why traditional static financial planning tools are insufficient to prepare for this risk. He proposes a four-component retirement system including the 5-year rule, dynamic guardrails-based spending strategies, a monitoring system, and ongoing retirement education.

title: The Truth About Retirement YouTube Videos (That No One Is Talking About)
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, critiques YouTube retirement case study videos, arguing they provide only declarative knowledge while real retirement success requires procedural knowledge gained through experience or guided practice. He outlines three main problems: oversimplification of complex decisions (using Roth conversions as a detailed example), hiding the hard work behind successful outcomes, and fueling unhealthy comparison to fictional scenarios.

title: Will My Dividend Portfolio Be Enough For My Retirement?
summary: Exposes why dividend-only investment strategies fail retirees by creating concentration risk, underspending, and false safety illusions. Argues that successful retirement planning requires diversified income strategies blending dividends, capital gains harvesting, and controlled principal drawdowns, guided by guardrails frameworks.

title: 2 Things to Do BEFORE You Retire at 60
summary: Eric Amzalag presents two critical retirement readiness pillars for age 60: income security (ensuring desired spending can be maintained throughout retirement using cash-flow models and risk-based guardrails rather than wealth-based metrics) and risk resilience (mitigating sequence-of-returns risk and surprise expenses through guardrail systems, historical market context, and stress-testing scenarios). Includes a four-step framework for evaluating personal retirement readiness.

title: Claim Social Security Early (Even If The Math Says Otherwise)
If You Want To Retire In The Next 12 Months... Watch This
The ROTH Conversion Lie ALL Retirees Believe
summary: Financial planner Eric Amzalag presents research-backed arguments against Roth conversions for most retirees, arguing that fear-driven recommendations ignore the time value of money and often expose early retirees to substantial withdrawal-rate risk without guaranteed tax benefits.

title: Your Savings Don't Matter: Here's The Secret To Your Dream Retirement
An HONEST Review Of What Retirement Looks Like With $2.2M
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, walks through the retirement plan of a hypothetical couple (William and Sherry Gallagher, both 60) with $2.2M saved, showing how guardrail-based planning software determines a safe spending capacity of $154,000/year and demonstrates resilience through historical market downturns including the GFC and dot-com crash.

title: Once I Show Retiree's This, They Stop Worrying About Spending...
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, explains why financially prepared retirees struggle to spend their wealth and introduces the Retirement Confidence Dashboard — a three-sheet tracking system for portfolio withdrawals, guardrails monitoring, and spending confidence — as the behavioral tool that replaces probability-of-success models and gives retirees actionable control.

title: 5 “Must Have” Purchases EVERY Retiree Regrets
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, walks through five seemingly prudent retirement purchases that frequently lead to regret: long-term care insurance, a second home, first-time investment property, rushed relocation, and paying off a mortgage just to be debt-free. Each is analyzed through real client examples to show how these decisions often reduce liquidity and flexibility more than they improve security.

title: The 4 Step System To Living Your Dream Retirement
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP and RICP, presents a four-step retirement planning framework: retirement assessment (financial and non-financial), retirement prescription (prioritized action items by planning phase), retirement monitoring (tracking key metrics with defined frequencies), and a retirement system for ongoing accountability. The video advocates for professional guidance over DIY planning and cites studies suggesting advisor-assisted strategies can improve retirement income by up to 38%.

title: What Actually Happens When You Overspend In Your First 5 Years in Retirement
Do These 5 Things BEFORE You Retire If You've Saved Over $1M
summary: Five essential pre-retirement actions for those with over $1M in savings: running detailed financial projections, addressing deferred medical/dental work, establishing fitness habits, securing credit access, and creating a loved ones financial checklist for family clarity.

title: The Fall of the 3 Bucket Strategy (And What’s Replacing It)
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, argues that the popular three-bucket retirement strategy is fundamentally flawed due to hidden market-timing assumptions and lack of systematic rebalancing rules, and proposes a four-layer guardrail framework as a superior alternative that uses asset sleeves, target weights, tolerance-band triggers, and a predefined rebalancing frequency.

title: Watch This Video If You're An American Retiring With A Pension
summary: Eric Amzalag explains how pension decisions are among the most critical but misunderstood retirement choices. Most retirees undervalue pensions or make irreversible decisions without understanding impact on withdrawal rates, spousal protection, and lifetime income.

title: If You Are This Retiree, Roth Conversions Are Not For You
summary: Critical analysis of Roth conversion strategies for different retiree wealth tiers. Explains that fear-based tax advice drives unnecessary conversions; only specific wealth categories benefit. $500K-below: rarely beneficial; $500K-$5M: narrowly applicable; $5M+: usually advantageous. Emphasizes context-dependent planning over universal solutions.

title: Can I Retire at 59.5 with $2M Without Running Out?
summary: Eric Amzalag walks through a detailed case study of Mark and Lisa, a California couple with $2.2M in savings asking if they can retire at age 59.5. Using Income Labs software, he addresses three critical decision points: managing 6-7% early withdrawal rates until Social Security, optimizing Roth conversion timing, and dynamically claiming Social Security to mitigate portfolio stress.

title: My Honest Advice to Couples Working Past 62… RETIRE NOW
summary: Financial planner argues couples who have saved adequately should retire at or after age 62 rather than delaying for additional growth. Personal anecdote about father's pancreatic scare illustrates gap between lifespan and healthspan; warns that pursuing higher scores prolongs work while health and attention decline—irreplaceable resources.

title: Give Me 8 Minutes, Retire With Zero Regrets
summary: Most retirement regrets are non-financial: lack of life design, deferred health, missing relationships and purpose. An Edward Jones study found 54% of retirees underplanned the life side of retirement. Video teaches fear-setting, stoic philosophy, and intentional decision-making to shift from drifting to designing retirement; emphasizes hard choices now enable easier, more fulfilling later years.

title: Already Retired? Here's What You Can Actually Spend
summary: CFP Eric Amzalag discusses how well-prepared retirees often end up spending too little due to fear and misaligned industry incentives. The video presents two solutions: guaranteed income sources (annuities/pensions) and financial education through comprehensive planning, illustrated through the case of Melody who increased annual spending from $50k to $160k with proper guidance.

title: My Honest Advice To Retirees, The 4% Rule Never Works...
summary: Eric Amzalag criticizes the 4% withdrawal rule as outdated and impractical, noting that less than 20% of retirees actually use it. He introduces dynamic guardrails as a superior alternative that adapts to market conditions, spending patterns, required minimum distributions, and end-of-life care costs in ways the rigid 4% rule cannot.

title: The Retirement System That Gets You Ahead of 97% of Retirees
summary: Eric Amzalag introduces the Eisenhower Matrix as a decision-making framework for retirement planning, showing how retirees often treat all decisions with equal urgency when they should prioritize based on importance. He categorizes retirement decisions into urgent-and-important, important-but-not-urgent, and less critical quadrants to reduce decision paralysis.

title: You Saved Too Much: Why Retirees With $1M Die With $5M
summary: Eric Amzalag addresses the consumption gap where retirees with over $1 million saved often end life with 3-5 times their starting wealth due to underspending fears. He presents two tools to increase spending confidence: guaranteed income sources (annuities/pensions) and financial education through dynamic planning, using Melody's case study of increasing from $50k to $160k annual spending.

title: Why Retiring at 55 vs 65 Completely Changes Your Retirement
summary: Explores the critical trade-offs between retiring at 55 versus 65, emphasizing that early retirement prioritizes health and vitality while later retirement offers greater financial certainty. Presents the concept of middle-ground solutions, such as career transitions or reduced work hours, to balance both health and financial security through systematic retirement planning.

title: Once You Understand This, You'll Avoid Roth Conversions
summary: Critically examines how financial planning software misleads retirees into unnecessary Roth conversions by assuming unrealistic longevity assumptions. Demonstrates that most retirees do not live long enough for conversions to provide benefits, and introduces a 'micro Roth conversion' strategy that bases conversions on actual spending capacity each year rather than software projections.

title: I am 62 with $1.4M. Can I Retire Now AND Spend $10k a month?
summary: Eric Amzalag analyzes whether a 62-year-old with $1.4 million in retirement savings can retire immediately and spend $10,000 monthly.

title: When I Show Retirees This, They Stop Worrying About RMDs
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, challenges the widespread fear around required minimum distributions by presenting four favorable regulatory changes since 2017 and a four-strategy playbook to manage RMDs. He argues that updated standard deductions, lower tax brackets, later RMD start ages, and smaller required percentages collectively reduce the RMD burden, and that the widow's tax trap only materially harms households with several million dollars in pre-tax accounts.

title: If You Don't Understand This, You Won't Retire Before 60
summary: Explains why intuition-based retirement planning fails for those in their 50s and introduces a four-step system for precise planning that can enable early retirement before age 60. Contrasts intuitive decision-making with systematic evaluation of baseline finances, spending speed limits, portfolio performance metrics, and feedback loops, showing how shifting from gut feeling to structured planning can retire years earlier.

title: Why Retirees With Under $5M Should Avoid Roth Conversions
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, argues that Roth conversions are frequently misrepresented as guaranteed wins and are in fact longevity bets with poor odds for most retirees. Using Income Labs software across $1 million, $3 million, and $5 million household case studies, he demonstrates that shortening the assumed lifespan by five years collapses projected Roth conversion benefits from six or seven figures to near zero, and provides a net-worth-tiered checklist for evaluating whether conversions make sense.

title: I’m a Retirement Expert. Here’s Why RMDs Don’t Matter
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, argues that required minimum distributions are a tax non-issue for over 99% of retirees, presenting modeled scenarios at $1 million and $3 million pre-tax savings levels to show that RMDs rarely push effective tax rates higher, and offers four strategies — blended withdrawals, asset location, micro Roth conversions, and qualified charitable distributions — for those who do need to manage them.

title: Give Me 82 Seconds, You’ll Avoid Roth Conversions Forever
summary: A brief educational video that identifies three critical mistakes financial planning tools make with Roth conversions: ignoring upfront tax bills which increase early withdrawal rates, assuming unrealistic longevity assumptions requiring 30+ years to recover, and treating conversions as guaranteed rather than risky investments. Proposes reversing the decision framework to determine sustainable spending first, then converting only if it increases lifetime spending or reduces risk.

title: Once I Show Retirees This, They Stop Worrying About Taxes
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, demonstrates five methods for achieving zero federal income tax in retirement by combining the standard deduction, the 0% long-term capital gains bracket, tax gain harvesting, tax lot selling, and tax loss harvesting.

title: You Need THIS Much To Retire & Spend $10k/Mo At Age 55/60/65
summary: Calculates the exact portfolio size needed to spend $10,000 per month after taxes when retiring at ages 55, 60, and 65, illustrating how retirement age and account type (pre-tax vs. taxable brokerage) dramatically affect required savings. Introduces the concept of asset location as a strategy that can reduce required savings by roughly $300,000 at any age.

title: The Hidden Social Security Math Nobody Shows Retirees
Once I Show Early Retirees This, Healthcare Becomes EASY
summary: Explains how modified adjusted gross income (MAGI) is the single variable controlling ACA health insurance premiums in early retirement, demonstrates how a couple spending $120,000 per year can legally pay zero or near-zero monthly premiums by blending withdrawals from pre-tax, Roth, and after-tax accounts, and warns against three common mistakes that destroy the strategy.

title: New Healthcare Rules Just Set a $50k Trap for ALL Retirees
The Hidden ROTH Math Nobody Shows Retirees
A Retirees Guide To Avoiding The $140,000 Pension Mistake
If You Are THIS Retiree, You Can Spend MORE Than You Think
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, outlines four levels of retirement spending—reactive spender, conservative planner (4% rule), autopilot glider, and adaptive optimizer—and argues that the guardrail-based adaptive optimizer delivers over $300,000 more spending in the first decade of retirement compared to the 4% rule while maintaining an 80% probability of success.

title: Once They Learn This, Retirees Stop Worrying About Taxes
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, walks through four tax strategies that can reduce a retiree's federal income tax bill from roughly $40,000 annually to zero without reducing spending: the income illusion (distinguishing spending from taxable income), strategic account withdrawal sequencing, tax gain harvesting to reset brokerage cost basis, and the Roth IRA as invisible income that avoids IRMAA charges and ACA premium increases.

title: Copy This Social Security Strategy, You’ll Die 10x Happier
When Early Retirees Do This, They Save $20,000 On Healthcare
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, explains the ACA subsidy cliff for pre-Medicare retirees — where household income crossing 400% of the federal poverty level causes health insurance premiums to jump by roughly $19,000 per year — and introduces the 'flexibility ratio' as a framework for measuring how much of a retiree's portfolio can be withdrawn without triggering taxable income, enabling strategic withdrawal sequencing to stay below the subsidy threshold.

title: If You're THIS Retiree, You Will Die Too Rich
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, argues that most retirees dramatically underspend because the financial services industry uses fear-based static rules like the 4% rule to keep assets under management. He presents research showing average withdrawal rates of 1.9–2.

title: The Hidden Investing Math NOBODY Shows Retirees
Do THIS Retiree Withdrawal Hack & Never Worry About Tax Again
summary: CFP Eric Amzalag presents a case study showing how early retirees with large pre-tax 401k balances and high-cost-basis taxable accounts can save over $450,000 in lifetime taxes by withdrawing from pre-tax accounts first—inverting conventional sequencing—to maintain ACA healthcare subsidies, reduce future RMDs, and mitigate the widow's tax trap, without doing Roth conversions.

title: Copy This NEW Retiree Spending Strategy, You’ll Die 10x Happier
Run This Calculator Once And Never Worry About RMDs Again
The Hidden Saving Math Nobody Shows Retirees
summary: The video reveals how retirement planning guidance significantly overstates the savings required. By separating Social Security income from portfolio withdrawal needs and accounting for tax and expense reductions, actual required savings are typically 40-50% lower than conventional wisdom suggests.

title: Copy this ROTH Strategy, You'll Avoid RMDs
summary: The video explains a tax-efficient retirement strategy called micro Roth conversions, which uses small amounts of surplus spending capacity each year to convert pre-tax retirement funds to Roth accounts, potentially saving hundreds of thousands in lifetime taxes while eliminating required minimum distributions without sacrificing lifestyle.

title: How Many Retirees ACTUALLY UNDERSPEND (And How To Stop It)
summary: The video reveals that 69% of affluent retirees do not withdraw systematically from their portfolios, with many still saving 31% of retirement income due to insufficient planning. The speaker presents a six-component retirement preparation framework beyond mere accumulation—scenario modeling, risk management, lifestyle design, family alignment, and psychological preparation—each requiring 15-20% of planning energy.

title: How Retirees With $1M+ At 65 Have $0 By 78
summary: The video explains sequence of returns risk, showing how market timing in early retirement determines success more than 30-year average returns. Research by Michael Kitsis demonstrates the first 15 years of retirement (correlation 0.91) drive outcomes, not the full 30-year average (correlation -0.09). A reverse glide path strategy starting conservative and becoming more aggressive reduces retirement failure risk.

title: The Hidden Cost Of Retirement NOBODY Tells Retirees
summary: Explores how most retirees fail to account for lumpy spending patterns in retirement. Demonstrates a two-tier spending system that separates stable baseline income from a flexible bonus bucket to handle unexpected large expenses and forgotten annual costs.

title: Copy This Social Security Hack & Never Worry About Tax Again
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, challenges the conventional advice to delay Social Security to age 70 in order to perform early Roth conversions, presenting a case study of a married couple with $1.5 million in pre-tax accounts who come out $122,000 ahead by claiming Social Security at 65 instead.

title: Can You Stop Funding Your 401(k) IMMEDIATELY?!
summary: Explains how compound growth can reach a milestone where retirement savings contributions become optional years before traditional retirement age. Uses a calculator-based approach to identify when investment growth alone will cover desired retirement spending.

title: Net Worth & Income of Top 1%, 5%, 10%, and 25% of Retirees (2026 Update)
summary: Analyzes retirement wealth distribution across income tiers using Federal Reserve data, revealing that successful retirees prioritize control over income sources and tax brackets rather than absolute net worth. Explores characteristics of top 1%, 5%, 10%, and 25% of retirees and presents three strategies used by the wealthiest.

title: Pay $0 Tax For a Decade? Use This Hidden Social Security Formula...
summary: Demonstrates how a married couple claiming Social Security at 62 can achieve zero federal income tax for a decade using the combined income formula, capturing over $20,000 in ACA health insurance subsidies and extracting nearly $150,000 from a pre-tax IRA tax-free through a phased withdrawal strategy.

title: This Boring Strategy Means ANYONE Can Retire With $2M
summary: Demonstrates how to safely achieve higher spending in early retirement through phased spending strategy combined with liability matching and guaranteed income. Shows a real example of a couple managing 30,000 dollars monthly for 5 years then dropping to 12,000 dollars annually with a 2.4 million dollar portfolio.

title: Don't Die With $2 Million You Were Too Scared To Spend
summary: Eric Amzalag explains the 'survive versus thrive gap' in retirement planning, where Monte Carlo probability-of-success tools measure only whether retirees won't run out of money, not whether they maintain their desired lifestyle. Research shows 2 out of 3 retirees underspend throughout retirement and die with double their starting wealth. The video advocates for dynamic spending tools like guardrails that provide turn-by-turn guidance instead of static survival scores.

title: Once Retirees Save $1.5M+, This Tax Hack Saves $100k+
summary: Eric Amzalag demonstrates a three-phase strategic withdrawal strategy for retirees with $1.5M+ that minimizes taxes through tax-bracket filling and capital gains harvesting. Over 10 years, a couple achieves a 1.68% effective tax rate while withdrawing $1.4M in taxable income, accessing over $170K annually while paying minimal federal taxes. The strategy involves strategic IRA and Social Security timing, harvesting long-term capital gains at 0% brackets, and resetting cost basis.

title: You Need THIS MUCH To Retire & Spend $12k/$10k/$8k A Month
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, presents a six-step framework for determining sustainable retirement spending, using a case study of a 58-year-old couple with $1.2 million seeking $10,000 per month. The framework prioritizes behavioral and asset-location levers over investment risk-taking and demonstrates how combining multiple changes — including optimizing asset location — can close large spending gaps.

title: Once You Have $2M Saved, Retire IMMEDIATELY (Here's Why)
summary: Eric Amzalag argues that $2 million is sufficient to retire immediately at age 62, using guardrails-based planning to show a 97% success rate over 33 years for a couple needing $95K annual spending. Working two additional years costs 480 days (5% of remaining healthy lifespan) to gain $250K—a poor trade. Healthcare before Medicare is addressed through ACA subsidies at low modified AGI, costing ~$700/month.

title: Once Retirees Save $2M+, This Is The BEST Withdrawal Strategy
summary: Eric Amzalag demonstrates why $2 million is the optimal threshold for a strategic withdrawal strategy combining tax-gain harvesting and IRA drawdown optimization. Over 10 years, a retired couple achieves cumulative tax savings of $233K through $44K in decade-one taxes, $80K in future RMD tax reductions, and $110K in eliminated capital gains taxes.

title: 4 Things to Know if You're Retiring and Single
summary: Examines four financial and non-financial penalties single retirees face versus married couples, including tax bracket disadvantages and caregiving costs, while highlighting advantages like simplicity in Social Security planning and estate decisions.

title: If You Knew What $2.1M Meant, You’d Retire TOMORROW
summary: Explains why accumulating over $2.1 million for retirement may be counterproductive due to loss aversion and healthspan-lifespan gaps. Argues that continuing to work past financial adequacy trades healthy retirement years for money that cannot be fully enjoyed later.

title: How To Do Roth Conversions (Without Paying More Taxes)
summary: Explores the break-even tax rate (BETR) framework from Vanguard research, showing how Roth conversions can make mathematical sense even when future tax rates are expected to decline, depending on account composition and time horizon.

title: 3  Reasons To Retire Tomorrow (Even If It Feels Too Soon)
summary: Presents three data-driven reasons for early retirement at 58 with $1.5M+: limited remaining time with family based on actuarial data, healthy life expectancy gap showing only 14 of 23 remaining years in good health, and rising health risk profiles that make delaying retirement a poor bet with irreplaceable time.

title: Once Retirees See This, They Stop Working
summary: A CFP walks through a complete retirement plan for a hypothetical couple (Mike, 59, and Laura, 61) with $2.05 million in assets, demonstrating live how to assess retirement readiness, optimize Social Security claiming, structure tax-efficient withdrawals across four retirement phases, and evaluate contingency risks using the Income Labs planning tool.

title: The Hidden IRA / 401(k) Math Nobody Shows Retirees
summary: Details a four-season withdrawal strategy for retirees with significant pre-tax accounts (IRAs/401ks), optimizing for ACA subsidies, Medicare penalties, and required minimum distributions to potentially save over 200K in taxes versus conventional approaches.

title: Watch This BEFORE You Turn 60
summary: Explains why age 60 is critical for retirement planning as the last year before multiple tax and income systems activate. Details progressive complexity: age 60 offers pure tax flexibility; age 62 adds Social Security and pension decisions; age 63 triggers Medicare IRMA lookback; age 65 brings Medicare; age 73 forces RMDs, closing the planning window.

title: One System That Makes TAX A Non-Issue For Retirees
summary: CFP Eric Amzalag walks through a systematic, four-season retirement tax planning framework using a detailed spreadsheet-based case study of a hypothetical couple with $4M in assets, showing how to evaluate ACA subsidy eligibility, execute Roth conversions, and harvest concentrated stock positions in a coordinated sequence to save nearly $900K in lifetime taxes.

title: The Government Is About To RESET Social Security (what you need to know)
summary: Video explains how the Claiming Age Clarity Act uses intentionally manipulative label changes (delayed retirement credits → maximum monthly benefit age, early eligibility age → minimum monthly benefit age) to subtly nudge people into claiming Social Security later without addressing the underlying 2034 trust fund depletion crisis.

title: Once You Understand This You’ll Stop Delaying Retirement
summary: Identifies a behavioral pattern that keeps pre-retirees stuck: certainty-seeking behavior that demands uncontrollable outcomes be resolved before acting. Contrasts this with a peace-of-mind-focused approach and introduces the Eisenhower matrix as a tool to sort retirement decisions by urgency and importance.

title: Why Retirees Who Plan Like This, End Up With Less To Spend
summary: Video argues that spending is the single most important variable in retirement planning, and that optimizing individual components (taxes, Social Security claiming, health insurance, retirement age) in isolation can undermine overall spending power due to interconnected tax cliffs and subsidy eligibility thresholds.

title: What Advisors Won't Tell You About Roth Conversions
summary: Retirement planning expert Eric Amzalag examines whether retirees can afford Roth conversions by analyzing spending capacity versus spending needs. The video demonstrates that affordability depends on having surplus spending capacity after retirement expenses are met, not simply on the size of retirement assets. Case studies show one household of $1.

title: 5 Years From Retirement? Here’s Exactly What To Do
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, presents a year-by-year retirement planning checklist covering the five years before retirement, arguing that starting from a clear life vision rather than tax optimization is the critical first step, and that early preparation prevents costly last-minute decisions.

title: Learn EVERYTHING About Early Retiree Healthcare In 29 Minutes
summary: Comprehensive guide to health insurance planning for early retirees (before age 65). Covers COBRA, spousal employer plans, short-term plans, and ACA marketplace options. Emphasizes that Modified Adjusted Gross Income (MAGI) is the critical control variable for ACA premium costs, with major income thresholds ($62,600 single / $84,600 married in 2026) that create dramatic subsidy cliffs.

title: What No One Tells You About Your Retirement Age (But I Will)
summary: Financial planner explains why standard retirement ages (62-70) are misleading and presents a personal satisfaction set point framework using guardrails strategy to determine when retirement is actually feasible for individual circumstances.

title: Everything Shifts After $1.5M In Savings, Here's How
summary: Video argues that at $1.5M in savings, most people have already reached retirement readiness threshold and that continuing to work to accumulate beyond this point creates diminishing quality-of-life returns while increasing tax complexity, RMD complications, and IRMAA surcharges. Emphasizes the true cost of delayed retirement is irreplaceable time during healthy, active years.

title: The Planning Lie That's Stealing Retirees' Best Years
summary: Video critiques traditional liabilities-first retirement planning approach that asks retirees to guess future spending, arguing instead for economics-based planning that calculates actual spending power first, then allows individuals to allocate within that figure, empowering retirees with concrete numbers rather than uncertain predictions.

title: The $30,000+ Retirement Expense Medicare Doesn’t Cover
summary: Explains IRMAA (Medicare Income-Related Monthly Adjustment Amount), a surcharge triggered by income from 2 years prior that can cost $2,300-$14,000+ annually. Details three scenarios causing unexpected IRMAA cliffs and provides tools for managing exposure.

title: Why Smart Retirees Are Quitting Traditional Planning
summary: Seven habits for retirement readiness combining financial discipline and personal development. Covers spending awareness, tracking, practice withdrawals, partner alignment, financial education, personal growth, and physical fitness.

title: Retirement Expert Explains If You Can Retire TOMORROW
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, outlines five milestones that determine retirement readiness: clarifying that Roth conversions are optimization tools not retirement enablers, calculating the portfolio income gap, having a concrete health insurance plan, building a tax-efficient withdrawal roadmap, and documenting a contingency plan for major market downturns. He argues that all five must be addressed before retiring confidently.

title: If You Have $1M+ In Pre-Tax, Once You Retire, DO THIS…
summary: CFP Eric Amzalag presents three case studies for retirees with $1M, $2M, and $3M+ in pre-tax accounts, demonstrating how strategic withdrawal sequencing and timing relative to ACA subsidy cliffs, IRMAA thresholds, and RMD windows can save six figures in taxes and healthcare costs.

title: Once You Retire, Look Poor, Before Telling Anyone
summary: Strategic retirement income planning to access government benefit programs through managing modified adjusted gross income. Covers ACA subsidies, Medicare Part D extra help, IRMA surcharges, and state property tax programs worth $30,000+ annually.

title: EVERYONE Picks The Wrong Social Security Age, Until They Learn This
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, argues that most couples choose suboptimal Social Security claiming ages because they frame claiming as a timing question rather than identifying the three jobs Social Security performs: guaranteed income, strategic flexibility as a triaging tool, and survivor protection. He presents a coordinated strategy—higher earner delays to 70, lower earner claims at 62—and walks through four scenarios, also covering the exception for high-wealth retirees above $5M.

title: Dangerously Honest Advice To Retire Before 59.5
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, presents a case study of a couple who used a SEPP 72(t) distribution strategy to enable retirement at ages 55 and 57, with $2 million entirely in pre-tax accounts, by reverse-engineering the required IRA balance needed to generate a $40,000 annual income gap using the RMD method — avoiding the 10% early withdrawal penalty before age 59.5.

title: Brutally Honest Advice For Anyone Still Waiting To Retire
summary: Uses a case study of a couple who believed they were four years from retirement to demonstrate how ignoring Social Security income, ACA healthcare subsidies through MAGI management, and early withdrawal penalty workarounds (Rule of 55 and 72T/SEPP) can allow early retirement sooner than expected.

title: Give Me 17 Minutes And I'll Protect Your Spouse When You Die
summary: Eric Amzalag outlines seven critical planning issues married couples face in retirement: the widow's penalty (tax bracket cliff for surviving spouse), survivor income optimization (Social Security and pension claiming), strategic Roth conversion timing, healthcare bridge gaps before Medicare, long-term care sequencing, step-up-in-basis and asset titling, and creating an operational system the surviving spouse can manage.

title: Do This In Your 60s And Never Worry About Taxes Again
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, introduces the 30-5-1 rule — a three-lens tax planning framework for retirees that combines a 30-year map of tax terrain, five-year phase-based objectives, and annual execution plans — to eliminate tax surprises in retirement rather than merely minimizing this year's tax bill.

title: Vanguard Says You Need THIS Much To Retire — Here’s Why They’re Wrong
summary: Critiques Vanguard's income replacement ratio approach to retirement planning, arguing that it causes retirees to overestimate required portfolio size. Uses a 62-year-old couple case study to show that spending-first modeling via guardrail software yields a retirement date 9.5 years earlier than the replacement ratio method suggests.

title: If All Your Savings Are Pre-Tax, Do This Before You Retire…
summary: Demonstrates how tax-efficient withdrawal sequencing in retirement can save $250,000-$500,000+ in taxes over 30 years by strategically using pre-tax, after-tax, and Roth accounts. Details five phases of retirement tax planning from pre-retirement through RMDs.

title: If you're 55+, with $2M saved, retire tomorrow...
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, makes the case that $2M in savings can support more retirement spending than most models suggest, and identifies the specific situations where it may still fall short. He cites research on declining retiree spending, income replacement rates, Social Security as a portfolio complement, dynamic withdrawal rates before and after Social Security begins, and concentrated long-term care costs as the main risk to a $2M plan.

title: Once Pre-Tax Millionaires Learn This, They Stop Worrying About Tax
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, analyzes at which pre-tax retirement account balances RMDs become a genuine tax problem versus media-driven fear. Using JP Morgan spending data and 2026 tax brackets, he models $5M, $3M, and $1M scenarios to show that the lifetime RMD tax drag is a seven-figure issue at $5M, potentially near seven figures at $3M, but relatively modest at $1M.

title: This Tax Hack Disappears The SECOND You Retire
summary: Pre-retirement tax strategy for high net worth early retirees: deliberately overpaying taxes by redirecting 401(k) contributions to Roth and taxable accounts produces 1.5-2x returns through ACA health insurance subsidies in early retirement years.

title: You Need THIS Much Roth To Stop Worrying About Tax (By Age)
summary: Retirement planning requires holding 10% of total assets in Roth accounts by age 60 to enable healthcare subsidy strategies. Typical retirees hold only 5-10% of needed amounts and must use Roth conversions or increased savings to build adequate post-tax balance.

title: This Is The EXACT Amount Of Roth You Need To Convert
summary: Financial advisor explains a systematic framework for determining the maximum Roth conversion amount that a retirement plan can afford based on surplus spending capacity rather than tax bracket optimization alone.

title: I'm 55 With $1M+ Saved, How Can I Retire By 60?
summary: Eric Amzalag, CFP, walks 55-year-olds targeting retirement at 60 through a three-part framework: establishing spending capacity versus desired spending to identify whether age 60 is feasible, optimizing the account mix and withdrawal sequence over the five-year runway, and stress testing the plan against five risk scenarios before committing to the retirement date.

title: 5 SHOCKING Early Retiree Healthcare Stats
summary: Financial advisor presents data on early retiree healthcare costs and subsidies, showing that marketplace coverage before Medicare is more affordable and accessible than many retirees assume.

title: Most Retirees Have NO Idea They Should Sell These Assets (Before They Retire)
summary: Financial advisor cautions against four common investment purchases made before retirement—bonds, rental properties, high-dividend funds, and late-life long-term care insurance—explaining how each can reduce portfolio flexibility when it matters most.

title: Can You Really Spend 8% In Retirement? Here’s The Truth
summary: Financial advisor explains how high withdrawal rates in early retirement can be sustainable when structured as temporary bridges, using guaranteed income start dates and declining spending patterns to reduce portfolio risk over time.

title: 60 With $1 Million How Much Can I Spend In Retirement
summary: CFP Kevin Lum analyzes retirement readiness for a 60-year-old couple with $1 million in savings, comparing traditional Monte Carlo probability-of-success planning to dynamic income strategies. Using RightCapital and IncomeLab software, he demonstrates how spending patterns and withdrawal flexibility affect retirement outcomes.

title: A Surging IRS Penalty Is Costing Billions. How to Avoid It.
summary: CFP Kevin Lum explains the surge in IRS underpayment penalties to $7 billion in 2023, driven by higher interest rates, and details how retirees and those doing Roth conversions can avoid penalties through quarterly estimated tax payments, safe harbor rules, and proper tax form filing (Form 2210, Schedule AI).

title: The BEST DIY Retirement Software? A CFP® Reviews New Retirement.
summary: CFP Kevin Lum provides a live walkthrough of New Retirement planning software, building a sample plan for a hypothetical couple (Phil and Claire) and comparing it to RightCapital. The video covers setting inflation and return assumptions, entering expenses and Social Security income, Roth conversion analysis, and an assessment of each platform's strengths and weaknesses for DIY investors versus advisor-managed clients.

title: The Most Important Number -- And Everyone Gets It Wrong
summary: Analysis of retirement spending as the most critical retirement planning variable. Reviews Bureau of Labor Statistics data on expense categories including housing, transportation, food, utilities, healthcare, and entertainment. Introduces Monarch Money software for expense tracking and categorizes expenses into essential, discretionary, and lifestyle buckets to support realistic retirement planning.

title: Top 3 Retirement Income Methods EXPLAINED!
summary: Kevin Lum, CFP, explains three approaches to generating retirement income — probability-based, safety-first, and bucket strategy — using the 'four L's' framework (longevity, lifestyle, liquidity, legacy) and illustrates each approach through case studies comparing portfolios with and without annuities.

title: How Much Can You Spend in Retirement? A Surprising Answer
summary: Kevin Lum walks through a detailed retirement spending analysis for a $2 million portfolio couple using dynamic income strategy software. He demonstrates how to calculate sustainable spending capacity, the value of tax-efficient Roth conversions, portfolio stress-testing, and the importance of distinguishing essential versus discretionary expenses in retirement spending plans.

title: Do you need a million dollars to retire? Real-Life Case Analysis
summary: Kevin Lum debunks the myth that you need $1 million to retire, walking through a real-life case of a single person with $300,000 in savings and $300,000 in home equity retiring at 67. Examines how pension and Social Security coverage affect retirement success, identifies major risks (long-term care, medical expenses, home repairs), and explores mitigation strategies including state safety nets, home equity, and insurance.

title: Why Looking Poor Is Important for Retiring Well
summary: Kevin Lum explains why living modestly and below one's means is critical for retirement success. He contrasts wealthy people who live humbly with those appearing wealthy but carrying debt, explores the hedonic treadmill (how purchases provide diminishing happiness returns), and outlines three benefits: freedom from dissatisfaction, financial freedom, and reduced stress through financial margin.

title: 48% of Retirees Will Require This Care: Is the INSURANCE worth it?
summary: CFP Kevin Lum examines whether long-term care insurance is worth purchasing, presenting statistics on care needs (48% of 65-year-olds will require paid care), comparing traditional and hybrid LTC policies, exploring alternatives like HSAs, home equity, and Medicaid, and offering a three-group framework for determining who should buy insurance. A live quoting tool demonstration is also included.

title: Massive Hack Puts You at Risk — Act Now!
summary: CFP Kevin Lum addresses a 2.9 billion record data breach and explains government protections against identity theft, including Social Security Administration mySocial Security accounts, IRS Identity Protection PINs, credit freezes, and fraud alerts. He details how criminals exploit stolen data and provides step-by-step instructions for implementing each protection measure.

title: Technology is Separating Advisors. It Could IMPACT You.
summary: Kevin Lum argues that technology is increasingly differentiating financial advisors by enabling sophisticated retirement, tax, and income optimization that was previously impractical. He demonstrates five tools — Right Capital, Holistiplan, Income Laboratory, Riskalyze, and Wealth.com — showing how each enhances planning quality and advises consumers on how to evaluate whether their advisor is keeping pace.

title: 3 Social Security Mistakes EVERYONE Makes (Real World Examples)
summary: Financial advisor Kevin Lum explains three critical mistakes people make with Social Security: claiming benefits too early, overlooking tax implications, and ignoring spousal/survivor benefits. He provides real-world examples using financial planning software to demonstrate the long-term financial impact of delaying benefits and optimal claiming strategies for couples.

title: The Most UNDERRATED Account to Minimize TAXES in Retirement
summary: Kevin Lum, CFP, demonstrates how a taxable brokerage account with long-term capital gains can function almost as tax-efficiently as a Roth IRA in retirement, using the case of a couple needing $120,000 per year. The video covers the mechanics of standard deductions, long-term capital gains tax rates at 0%, qualified versus ordinary dividends, and shows scenarios where retirees can withdraw $125,000 in long-term gains completely federal tax-free, particularly when delaying Social Security.

title: This Will Change How You View Annuities Forever
summary: Kevin Lum explains why annuities, despite their bad reputation, may be optimal for some retirees seeking guaranteed income and behavioral confidence in retirement. Discusses conflicts of interest in financial advice (commission-based vs. fee-only models), when to consider annuities, and refinancing options for existing annuities in a higher-rate environment.

title: 80% of Retirees Make This Mistake - It's Easy to Avoid
summary: Kevin Lum, CFP, discusses a 2021 JP Morgan Chase study finding that 80% of retirees wait until forced RMDs before withdrawing from tax-deferred accounts, a strategy that leads to significantly higher lifetime taxes. He models how early proactive withdrawals reduce account balances, keeping future RMDs within lower tax brackets, and explains the Social Security tax torpedo that amplifies the cost of delayed withdrawals.

title: 7 Essential Accounts You Can't Afford to Ignore
summary: Kevin Lum, CFP, enumerates seven essential financial accounts for organized retirement planning — checking, high-yield savings or cash management, HSA, work retirement plan (401k/403b), traditional IRA, Roth IRA, and taxable brokerage — and pairs each with practical strategies including treasury-based tax savings, the HSA receipt reimbursement trick, backdoor Roth contributions, and asset location principles.

title: Your Retirement Plan Is Missing a CRUCIAL Element
summary: Kevin Lum, CFP, explores Abraham Maslow's hierarchy of needs and its application to retirement planning. The video discusses how successful retirement requires more than financial security—it demands fulfilling deeper human needs including relationships, self-esteem, learning, and transcendence through purposeful service to others.
