summary: James Conole, CFP, analyzes the retirement readiness of a hypothetical couple (Tommy and Monica) with a $2.2 million portfolio, showing through financial planning software that they can retire at 65 rather than 70 and still maintain their lifestyle, or alternatively increase spending or stop saving while remaining financially secure.

title: The Benefits of a Retirement Community and How to Find Your Purpose in Retirement
summary: James Conole interviews Joe Sullivan, a retired Air Force veteran and former HR professional, about his retirement journey. Sullivan shares how the first year and a half of retirement in rural Virginia proved emotionally challenging despite financial security, leading him to move to The Villages retirement community in Florida.

title: The 5 Biggest Risks to Your Retirement (and How to Insure Against Them)
summary: James Conole, CFP, outlines five major retirement risks—taxes, longevity, poor market returns, major health events, and inflation—and presents one concrete insurance strategy for each, including Roth conversions, maximizing Social Security, annuities, appropriate health and long-term care coverage, and stock ownership.

title: "The Joy Is Trying New Things and Being Surprised by What Works"
summary: Interview with Chris, a 58-year-old retiree who left a 31-year utility career, covering his emotional and practical experience 14 months into retirement. Topics include stress reduction after leaving work, finding purpose through mentorship and community service, the challenge of losing workplace relationships, and the benefit of one spouse retiring first to test the lifestyle before the other follows.

title: 5 Dangerous Rules of Thumb to Rely on in Retirement
summary: James Conole, CFP, challenges five widely repeated retirement planning rules of thumb—save 10x income by 67, save 10-15% of income, follow the 4% withdrawal rule, assume 10% average stock market returns, and subtract age from 100 for stock allocation. Each rule is shown to be dangerously oversimplified because it ignores individual spending, income sources, timing, sequence of returns risk, and the inflation risk of overly conservative portfolios.

title: Facing Health Challenges in Retirement and Learning to Reconnect with Your Spouse
summary: An interview-format episode of the Ready for Retirement podcast features retired federal law enforcement officer Derek Tinky discussing how his father's cancer-related financial ruin shaped his healthcare planning decisions, how he and his wife Stephanie navigated health crises shortly after retirement, and how deliberate pre-retirement conversations helped strengthen their marriage during the transition.

title: I have $2.3M in IRAs. How Do I Minimize Taxes in Retirement?
summary: Case study retirement makeover with Andre, age 56, who plans to retire in five years with over $2.6 million mostly in pre-tax accounts. The video walks through income projections, Roth conversion strategy, IRMAA surcharges, ACA health insurance subsidies, required minimum distributions, capital gains brackets, and employee stock compensation, illustrating how accurate expense planning must precede tax optimization decisions.

title: Unexpected Retirement: Finding Happiness and New Adventures
summary: Interview with Elaine about her unexpected retirement at age 57 after a company downsizing. After an initial identity crisis and boredom, she found fulfillment through pickleball, volunteer work, family time, and new relationships over the course of a year, ultimately reporting greater happiness in retirement than during her 25-year corporate career.

title: How to Reduce Sequence of Return Risk | 2 Essential Strategies for Early Retirement
summary: CFP James Conole walks a 52-year-old client (John) through a detailed retirement planning session focused on two levers for reducing sequence-of-return risk in early retirement: maintaining a sustainable withdrawal rate and reducing portfolio concentration away from US equities. Using John's real balance sheet and hypothetical S&P 500 vs.

title: Retire Happy: Build a Personalized Retirement Plan that You Can't Wait to Live Out
summary: Interview with Monique, a retiree who left a 30-year finance career at age 56, discussing two years of early retirement experience including challenges with transitioning from saver to spender, family travel expectations, health improvements, and personal transformation. Monique shares how a toxic workplace culture and health decline triggered her retirement planning, and how a structured daily schedule helps her thrive in retirement.

title: I'm 61 Years Old with $500k. Can I Retire?
summary: CFP James Conole conducts a live retirement makeover for David, a 61-year-old with roughly $530,000 in savings, no mortgage, and a pending pension and Social Security. The analysis covers withdrawal rates, sequence of return risk mitigation using a stock/bond separation strategy, ACA subsidy optimization, Social Security timing as a dynamic lever, and Monte Carlo projections showing David can retire immediately on a $2,400/month budget with high probability of success.

title: How to Overcome Fears of Spending in Retirement
summary: Interview with Ben, a retiree who retired at 53 after aggressive saving, discussing the psychological challenge of transitioning from saving to spending in retirement. Ben's portfolio has nearly doubled since retiring yet he struggled to spend it, partly due to chasing ACA health insurance subsidies.

title: Can We Still Retire if We Don't Receive the $750k Inheritance We're Expecting?
summary: CFP James Conole works through a retirement readiness analysis for Matt and Beth, a couple aged 57 planning to retire at 59.5, examining how a projected $750,000 inheritance, part-time income, real estate transitions, and early high withdrawal rates interact to determine whether their plan is viable and how it holds up if the inheritance fails to materialize.

title: Should We Always Pull From our Brokerage Account First in Retirement? (Hint: No)
summary: Analysis of optimal retirement withdrawal strategies for Patrick and Mary's situation, challenging conventional brokerage-first approach in favor of tax-gain harvesting and state income exclusions to preserve Roth and taxable account growth.

title: How to Avoid the RMD Tax Trap: Protect Your Lifetime of Tax Savings
summary: James Conole walks Spencer, a 48-year-old tech professional planning to retire at 50, through a retirement makeover focused on required minimum distributions (RMDs). The video analyzes how pre-tax 401(k) savings could generate large taxable RMDs at age 75, and compares Roth conversion strategies — filling the 12% and 22% brackets — against doing nothing, showing meaningful projected tax savings.

title: Restricted Stock Units: The Basics of RSUs and How to Use Them
summary: James Conole, CFP at Root Financial Partners, explains how restricted stock units (RSUs) work, how they are taxed, and how employees should think about whether to hold or sell vested shares. He argues that RSU vests should be treated as a cash bonus — prompting employees to ask what they would do with equivalent cash — rather than as a reason to maintain concentrated single-stock exposure. He also covers withholding mechanics, wash sale rules, and the risk of chasing recent stock performance.

title: RETIREMENT MAKEOVER: The Roth Conversion Trap: Common Tax Planning Mistakes to Avoid
summary: Retirement Makeover case study featuring Alex and Jeanette, a recently retired couple with approximately $4.4 million in assets plus a pension. CFP James Conole demonstrates how aggressive Roth conversion strategies could generate up to $1.3 million in tax-adjusted savings, but argues that optimizing for tax efficiency alone is a trap if it comes at the expense of quality of life and meaningful spending during prime retirement years.

title: The Complete Guide to Maximizing Your Social Security Benefit
summary: James Conole, CFP, provides a comprehensive guide to maximizing Social Security benefits, explaining FICA funding mechanics, the 35-year earnings calculation, bend points in the benefit formula, and four key strategies: working 35 years, delaying benefits to age 70, leveraging spousal benefits (up to 50% of a spouse's primary insurance amount), and optimizing survivor benefits where a surviving spouse receives 100% of the deceased's benefit.

title: 3 Simple Ways to Minimize Taxes in Retirement
summary: Case study of Elizabeth (53, retired) and Joe (51, retiring at 55) demonstrating three tax-efficiency strategies for retirement. Using $4.2M combined net worth and $8k/month spending goal, video shows how asset location, withdrawal sequencing, and Roth conversions can generate $3M+ in additional tax-adjusted assets and enhance retirement outcomes.

title: This Is the Single Most Important Thing You Can Do to Prepare for Retirement
summary: James Conole advocates that the most important retirement preparation is not financial optimization but clarifying one's values and life purpose through journaling. He guides listeners through exercises to identify what truly matters, envision a life without constraints, define retirement fears, and determine actionable next steps.

title: Retirement Risks: 7 Traps to Avoid
summary: James Conole analyzes early retirement risks through the case study of Chris and his wife, who have $4.7 million at age 40 and plan to retire in one year. The video identifies seven core retirement risks: market drops, tax increases, Social Security reduction, longevity, inflation, healthcare cost escalation, and lower asset returns.

title: 5 Things People Regret About Early Retirement
summary: Identifies five common regrets among people who retire early before age 65: not knowing what they're retiring to, adopting overly conservative portfolios, not considering part-time work transitions, not practicing retirement beforehand, and misapplying conventional retirement rules without understanding context.

title: Retirement Makeover: I'm 51 with $600k. When Can I Retire?
summary: Financial planner James Conole reviews the retirement prospects of Hillary (51) and Mark (55), who have roughly $600k saved and want to retire within 10 years to travel the world. Conole disputes advice from a previous adviser that they must work until 70, showing cash-flow projections that support retirement at 61/57 with a high probability of success, particularly if they sell their home and live abroad.

title: 3 Steps to Prepare for an Out-of-State Retirement
summary: James Conole outlines three critical considerations for retirees planning to move to a different state: comparing housing costs and property taxes, understanding differences in overall cost of living, and implementing a tax strategy aligned with the new state's tax laws. He demonstrates how seemingly equal moves can result in significant financial impacts through capital gains taxes, property tax rate differences, and varying state income tax treatments of retirement income.

title: How Does Rental Real Estate Impact Your Retirement Strategy?
summary: James Conole, CFP, walks through a retirement planning case study with Mark, a 62-year-old who owns three rental properties and plans to work until 70. The video covers how to measure rental real estate returns using income, appreciation, leverage, and tax benefits, then models how rental income combined with Social Security creates a strong retirement income floor that reduces portfolio withdrawal rates to under 1% annually.

title: 7 Things You Need to Know About Roth IRAs to Maximize Tax-Free Income
summary: James Conole, CFP, explains seven key Roth IRA nuances that go beyond basic contribution awareness: contribution limits, penalty-free access to contributions, the five-year rule for earnings, income limits and backdoor Roth strategies, no required minimum distributions, exclusion from provisional income calculations, and exclusion from IRMAA Medicare surcharge calculations.

title: Here's How To Discuss Money Effectively With Your Parents
summary: James Conole and Ari Taublieb, both CFPs at Root Financial, launch a joint podcast episode addressing how to initiate money conversations with aging parents. The video covers strategies for broaching sensitive topics like estate planning, long-term care, and inheritance, emphasizing the importance of leading with care for parents' wellbeing rather than interest in assets.

title: Roth Conversion Strategies for Singles: How Much Should You Convert?
summary: Financial planner James Conole walks through a retirement tax planning case study with Laura, a 60-year-old single retiree with approximately $2.2 million primarily in pre-tax accounts. The video covers Roth conversion strategy decisions, health insurance subsidy trade-offs, projected withdrawal rates, tax bracket optimization, and estate planning considerations for single filers.

title: How Soon Before Retirement Should You Adjust Your Portfolio?
summary: James Conole explains when to transition from growth-oriented stock portfolios to conservative allocations as retirement nears, emphasizing that timing depends on when funds will be needed rather than retirement date alone, and considering both financial capacity and psychological tolerance for volatility.

title: The Downside of Working "Just One More Year"
summary: Discussion on 'goalpost planning'—the tendency to delay retirement by continuously pushing back retirement timelines. Examines how people often use financial optimization as procrastination rather than addressing the harder emotional question of finding meaning in retirement. Presents a case study of teachers weighing part-time work, pensions, and travel against a preset retirement timeline.

title: Retirement Makeover: I Have a $2M Portfolio and I'm Burned Out at Work - When Can I Retire?
summary: CFP James Conole reviews the retirement readiness of Stacy and Michael, a couple with a $2M portfolio who started serious saving at age 47 and now want to retire at 54. The analysis covers withdrawal rates, portfolio concentration in employer stock, Social Security timing, healthcare costs, and spending optimization to help Stacy retire in 93 days.

title: This is the Biggest Way People Waste Money in Retirement
summary: James Conole uses the story of a multi-million-dollar retiree hesitant to furnish her home to explore why financially secure people struggle to spend. The video covers the diminishing marginal utility of wealth, the increasing value of time with age, and how frugality mindsets formed in scarcity can impede meaningful spending in retirement.

title: STOP! Why You Shouldn't Do a Roth Conversion
summary: Financial education on when not to pursue Roth conversions, covering lower future tax brackets, qualified charitable distributions, required minimum distributions, IRMAA thresholds, spending strategies, and the social security tax torpedo effect.

title: 3 Critical Retirement Planning Changes When You Have an Age Gap
summary: James Conole walks through retirement planning for age-gap couples using Daniel (53) and Dione (64) as a case study, identifying three critical planning changes: accounting for the younger spouse's extended longevity, planning for premature death of the higher-earning spouse, and stress-testing for long-term care events that could deplete assets available to the surviving spouse.

title: Unconventional Retirement Strategy: How to Save Less AND Retire with More
summary: James Conole, CFP, argues that for some clients aggressive retirement saving past a certain point is counterproductive, using a client case study to illustrate how redirecting savings toward present enjoyment can extend working years voluntarily and improve both quality of life and long-term financial outcomes.

title: 5 Things Happy Retirees do Daily
summary: Identifies five daily habits of happy retirees based on observations across hundreds of retirees: daily reflection and journaling, staying physically active, traveling and exploring new places, spending time with loved ones, and pursuing hobbies and interests. Each habit is explained through psychological, physical, and social benefits, with references to research and behavioral frameworks.

title: Top 5 Most Avoidable Retirement Mistakes
summary: James Conole identifies five common and highly avoidable retirement mistakes: spending the wrong amount (more often too little than too much), retiring at the wrong time, focusing on only one type of investment risk, providing excessive financial support to adult children, and failing to develop a retirement strategy. Each mistake is explained with behavioral and practical dimensions, along with how to guard against it.

title: I’m 60 with $2M How Much Can I Spend in Retirement?
summary: Case study of Joe and Jackie, a couple both aged 59 with $1.8 million in investments and $585,000 in property, analyzing retirement readiness at age 60 with $8,000 monthly expenses plus healthcare, property costs, and $25,000 annual travel. Initial withdrawal rates of 7-10% and a 33% probability of success indicate retirement is not immediately sustainable without adjustments such as working longer, reducing spending, or leveraging property assets.

title: Why You Should Avoid Simplistic Monte Carlo Results and Create a Real Financial Plan Instead
summary: Critical analysis of Monte Carlo simulations as financial planning tools, explaining why probability-of-success metrics alone are insufficient for comprehensive retirement planning. Contrasts simplified Monte Carlo analysis with holistic financial planning covering spending, income strategies, tax optimization, asset allocation, and lifestyle goals.

title: I’m 61 with $1.7M How Much Can I Spend in Retirement?
summary: James Conole walks through a detailed retirement analysis for Robin, a 61-year-old with $1.7 million in investments, a $40,000 pension, and planned Social Security at 67. The case study covers cash flow projections, withdrawal rate analysis, Monte Carlo simulation results showing a 78% success probability, and how to interpret failure severity given Robin's pension and Social Security as backstops.

title: 5 Biggest Retirement Fears and How to Overcome Them
summary: Identifies the five most common retirement fears — outliving savings, losing purpose, inadequate healthcare coverage, loneliness, and unaffordable long-term care — and provides concrete financial and behavioral strategies to address each, including Social Security optimization, HSA use, withdrawal guardrails, and long-term care insurance evaluation.

title: Retiring with Only Pre-Tax Accounts? Make These 3 Retirement Changes
summary: Tax optimization strategies for retirees with primarily pre-tax retirement accounts (IRAs, 401ks). Case study of Ruben and Katie ($3 million in pre-tax accounts) demonstrates three critical areas: adjusted withdrawal rates accounting for taxes, strategic Roth conversions and Qualified Charitable Distributions to reduce tax liability, and legacy planning considerations for high-income heirs. Shows potential to add $300,000+ to after-tax wealth through tax-efficient strategies.

title: How to Travel the Country and Start Living Before Actually Being Fully Retired
summary: LJ and Kelly describe how they traveled the U.S. for a full year before formal retirement, staying in Airbnbs for month-long stints. The video covers the financial logistics of domestic slow travel, healthcare planning on the road, the PT perspective on retiring healthy, and advice on cultivating hobbies and a purposeful retirement.

title: What if Social Security Gets Cut 3 Things All Retirees Should Do Now
summary: James Conole, CFP, outlines a three-step framework for assessing retirement preparedness in the event of a projected 20% Social Security benefit cut. Steps involve calculating discretionary versus essential expenses, determining Social Security's share of total income, and evaluating portfolio withdrawal rates to gauge how much margin exists to absorb a reduction.

title: 3 Simple Steps to Retire in 2025
summary: James Conole, CFP of Root Financial, presents a three-step framework for making 2025 the year someone actually retires: assess current financial health by inventorying assets, net income, and debts; envision what an ideal retirement looks like and estimate its cost; then connect those two pictures by determining whether current savings can sustainably fund the desired lifestyle. The 4% rule is introduced as a baseline for calculating a target portfolio size.

title: Here’s Why Working One More Year is the Biggest Retirement Decision You’ll Ever Make
summary: James Conole, CFP, walks through Kurt and Eva's retirement dilemma — whether to retire now at 67 or work one more year — using financial planning software to model probability of success, Social Security delayed credits, and portfolio growth.

title: How Should You Invest $10M+? Most Underutilized Strategy Revealed
summary: Educational overview of direct indexing — owning individual stocks that constitute an index rather than an ETF — as a tax-optimization strategy for high-net-worth investors.

title: Here’s Exactly How Much You Should Convert to Your Roth IRA to fill up the 22% Tax Bracket
Major Social Security Bill Passed: $190B in New Benefits to be Paid After Eliminating WEP & GPO
Roth Conversion Checklist: 5 Key Factors to Maximize Your Conversion Strategy
summary: James Conole, CFP, walks through five factors that determine optimal Roth conversion strategy using a case study of a 65-year-old couple with $4 million in assets. Beyond ordinary income tax brackets, he covers IRMAA Medicare surcharges, long-term capital gains brackets, terminal tax rates for heirs, and the importance of reviewing and potentially stopping conversions rather than continuing indefinitely.

title: 5 Advanced Social Security Strategies to Maximize Your Retirement Income
summary: James Conole, CFP, explains five Social Security limits critical for maximizing retirement benefits: the earnings limit (benefit withholding for pre-FRA earners), the wage base (taxable earnings cap), the maximum benefit amounts at ages 62/FRA/70, provisional income thresholds that determine how much of Social Security is taxable, and bend points that define the progressive benefit formula.

title: Exactly How Much Should You Save for Retirement
summary: James Conole, CFP, walks through two methods for calculating how much to save for retirement: a simple spreadsheet-based approach using the 4% withdrawal rule, and a detailed financial planning software approach that accounts for taxes, uneven spending, the retirement spending smile, and life expectancy.

title: Why We Just Launched A NEW Retirement Community: Root Collective
summary: Announces the launch of Root Collective, a paid online retirement community from Root Financial Partners, designed to extend access to peer connection, expert guests, and retirement education beyond traditional one-on-one financial advising. Covers the rationale, features, pricing structure, and target audience for the community.

title: No Taxes on Social Security? Here’s What Trump’s Plan Means for You
The Root Collective Is Now Free. Here's Why.
summary: Announces that Root Collective, a newly launched community platform, is now free after initial $50/month launch. Explains the rationale that a true community thrives when members connect directly with each other rather than needing constant institutional guidance, making accessibility more important than revenue.

title: I’m Single and 5 Years Out From Retirement. What Should I be Doing? (5 Specific Steps)
summary: James Conole, CFP at Root Financial, presents a five-step retirement readiness framework using a case study of Jenny, a 60-year-old single woman with $702k in assets aiming to retire at 65 on $6,000 per month, demonstrating how following the steps in the correct order raises her probability of success from 24% to 85% without requiring her to work longer.

title: The Most Important Thing You Can Do For Your Financial Plan
summary: Argues that financial security requires intentional living and value alignment, not perpetual accumulation. Challenges the hedonic treadmill where people always feel they need double their current wealth, instead encouraging readers to identify meaningful purchases or experiences and create a concrete plan to fund them now.

title: The Roth Conversion Mistake that Could DOUBLE (or more!) Your Medicare Costs
summary: Demonstrates how Roth conversions that appear optimal by ordinary income tax bracket can inadvertently trigger IRMAA Medicare surcharges, illustrating through a case study that a smarter conversion strategy staying below IRMAA thresholds increased projected tax-adjusted assets by roughly $275,000. Also covers five key tax implications to consider when planning Roth conversions.

title: Retire Now If you Answer "Yes" to These 5 Questions
summary: Presents five key questions to assess retirement readiness: whether time is slipping away, health is suffering, relationships are being neglected, health span (not just lifespan) is being optimized, and financial resources are sufficient. Argues that fixation on financial projections can cause people to defer retirement indefinitely despite meeting all readiness criteria.

title: How Much Can I Spend in Retirement with a $2M Portfolio
summary: James Conole, CFP of Root Financial Partners, walks through a detailed retirement cash flow analysis for a hypothetical couple, John and Sarah, both age 60 with a $2 million portfolio and $3.5 million net worth. The video demonstrates how income sources, spending goals, withdrawal rates, and goal-alignment can be modeled to show earlier retirement and higher spending are often more feasible than clients expect.

title: One Simple Tip to Make Spending Easy in Retirement
summary: James Conole, CFP, of Root Financial addresses the paradox that disciplined savers often struggle most with spending money in retirement.

title: Retiring Before You Start Social Security? Make This Crucial Portfolio Adjustment
summary: Uses a case study of Becky, a 62-year-old retiring with a $1 million 401k before Social Security begins at 67, to demonstrate why a pre-Social-Security retirement requires shifting from a 100% stock portfolio to approximately a 70/30 allocation, insulating five years of projected withdrawals from market volatility while improving the probability of retirement success.

title: The Best Retirement Spending Stories I've Heard This Year
summary: Showcases listener responses to a challenge to identify and fund one meaningful purchase or experience for the current year. Stories range from ice fishing trips and family vacations to cruises and pet adoption, illustrating how intentional spending aligned with values creates lasting fulfillment and acknowledging mortality's urgency.

title: Roth Conversions: When Will You Actually Break Even?
summary: Explains Roth conversion strategy break-even analysis using tax-adjusted asset value rather than nominal dollar amounts. Demonstrates with Luke and Mary's case how filling the 22% tax bracket until age 72 could save $3M in taxes and increase after-tax wealth by $3.3M, showing benefits materialize sooner when measured correctly.

title: Roth IRA Tax-Free Withdrawals (5-Year Rule Explained)
summary: Explains the five-year rules for Roth IRA tax-free withdrawals, covering three funding sources: contributions (always accessible), conversions (each with separate five-year clock), and growth (requires 59.5+ age). Demonstrates through examples how IRS assumes withdrawal order and why early retirees should strategically plan conversions.

title: Here's How to Pay Zero Taxes on a $100k Retirement Income (Easier Than You Think)
summary: James Conole demonstrates step-by-step how a retired couple — Joe and Sally — can generate $100,000 of annual income while paying zero federal income taxes by strategically combining Social Security, qualified dividends, IRA distributions, and long-term capital gains harvesting, leveraging the 0% capital gains bracket and the standard deduction.

title: 9 Things To STOP DOING After 60
summary: Financial planner James Conole presents nine behavioral and financial habits people should stop doing after age 60 to maximize retirement enjoyment. He emphasizes shifting from accumulation to living, prioritizing health and experiences, caring less about others' expectations, and making intentional decisions to enjoy the present rather than deferring gratification.

title: Here's Why So Many People Regret Retiring at 65
summary: Argues retiring at 65 may be a mistake by distinguishing between life expectancy and healthy life expectancy. US healthy life expectancy is 66.1 years versus 74 in Japan. Uses Scott and Brooke's retirement plan to show how financial projections ignore health constraints. Recommends prioritizing health, pursuing activities now, and planning earlier retirement.

title: Why many retirees REGRET relying on Social Security Income
summary: Explains four reasons why relying too heavily on Social Security is risky: one-time expenses aren't covered, CPIW inflation adjustments lag and underweight housing/healthcare costs, provisional income thresholds cause increasing taxation of benefits, and dependency creates anxiety. Recommends maximizing benefits, building investment portfolios, and leveraging home equity as contingencies.

title: How Will Your Spending Change Throughout Retirement
summary: Examines how retirement spending patterns change over 30+ years, with research showing retirees spend 20-25% less in real terms during later retirement years. Discusses income planning, portfolio sequencing, and how social security timing affects withdrawal strategies.

title: The Hidden Flaw in Monte Carlo Analysis That's Ruining Retirement Plans
summary: Critical examination of Monte Carlo analysis in retirement planning, questioning whether pursuing a 99-100% probability of success is actually optimal. Uses a case of a recently divorced 60-year-old whose success rate dropped from 99% to 70%, leading to a 10-year work extension. Argues that context matters more than a single number, and that 100% success often means sacrificing life's meaningful experiences.

title: The 3 Biggest Tax Mistakes I See Retirees Make
summary: James Conole, CFP, outlines three major tax mistakes retirees make: ignoring the 0% long-term capital gains bracket for tax gain harvesting, underestimating the Social Security tax torpedo caused by provisional income, and either under-converting or over-converting to Roth IRAs. Each mistake is illustrated with detailed case studies showing the tax and wealth impact.

title: Maximize Your Retirement Tax Savings: How to Maximize Tax-Saving for Each Account
summary: Comprehensive guide to tax optimization strategies for different retirement account types. Covers brokerage accounts with 0% capital gains brackets, 401(k)s and Roth options, HSAs triple tax benefits, inheritance step-up basis, inherited IRA distributions, and Social Security taxation. Demonstrates how strategic withdrawal sequencing can minimize lifetime tax liability.

title: Retirement Revolution: The Paradigm Shift That's Changing Everything
summary: James Conole, CFP of Root Financial, argues that a financial plan should function as a vision board in action, connecting investment assets to personal dreams rather than existing as a standalone set of numbers. Using a case study of retirees Brad (63) and Vicki (61), he demonstrates how goals like surf trips, hiking adventures, charitable giving, and family support are translated into a withdrawal and tax plan.

title: How Much Do I Need to Retire? (3 Simple Steps to Determine the Right Amount)
summary: James Conole presents a three-step framework for determining how much a retirement portfolio must be: calculate true retirement expenses (not current income), subtract non-portfolio income such as Social Security or pensions to find the gap, then divide the gap by an appropriate withdrawal rate (commonly 4–5.5%) to size the portfolio. Nuances including uneven income timing, tax treatment differences between income sources, and the survivor's benefit reduction upon a spouse's death are covered.

title: How Much Can I Spend if I Retire at 60 with $2M?
summary: Case study examining retirement spending strategy for retiring at 60 with $2M portfolio. Demonstrates why the simplistic 4% rule combined with Social Security timing creates uneven income flows. Proposes targeting desired after-tax spending amount and working backward to determine required withdrawals, accounting for taxes and delayed Social Security benefits to optimize retirement cash flow.

title: The COSTLY Mistake You're Making When Realizing Investment Gains
summary: James Conole, CFP, presents a framework for deciding when to realize taxable investment gains in a brokerage account, emphasizing that concentration risk from not selling often exceeds the tax cost. He also covers five strategies to reduce or eliminate long-term capital gains tax: utilizing the 0% bracket, gifting appreciated shares to charity, gifting appreciated shares to lower-bracket family members, benefiting from step-up in basis at death, and tax-loss harvesting.

title: Here's What Happens When You Draw Down Your 401k To Increase Social Security (Surprising)
summary: Analysis of Social Security claiming strategy showing the flaw in treating 8% annual increases as investment returns. Case study demonstrates how portfolio returns and opportunity costs affect break-even calculations when comparing early claiming versus delayed claiming strategies across ages 62-70.

title: The Most Powerful Tax-Free Retirement Tool: Start Leveraging Your HSA Now
summary: James Conole, CFP®, breaks down Health Savings Accounts (HSAs) as powerful tax-deferred investment vehicles with triple tax benefits: tax-free contributions, tax-free growth, and tax-free qualified withdrawals. He explains eligibility, contribution limits, the critical ability to decouple medical expense incurrence from withdrawal timing, and strategies to maximize tax-free retirement income by tracking and deferring medical reimbursements.

title: Maximize Your Roth IRA: When Should You Spend Your Roth Money in Retirement?
summary: CFP James Conole explains when to withdraw from Roth IRAs in retirement, presenting a framework that compares current tax brackets against future projected income including Social Security and Required Minimum Distributions to optimize tax-free withdrawals.

title: We have a $3M Retirement Portfolio. How Much Can We Spend Without Running Out of Money?
summary: James Conole, CFP, walks through a retirement planning case study for a couple (Jeffrey and Cindy, ages 66 and 64) with $3 million in portfolio assets. The video demonstrates how to calculate sustainable spending by analyzing cash flows, withdrawal rates, and probability of success, and argues that the planning framework applies regardless of portfolio size.

title: Social Security at 62 vs 67 vs 70: When should you start claiming your benefits?
summary: Comprehensive comparison of Social Security claiming strategies at ages 62, 67, and 70, analyzing trade-offs between immediate income access, benefit reductions, tax efficiency, and longevity risk. Examines how collection timing affects survivor benefits, spousal benefits, portfolio sustainability, and tax planning strategies like Roth conversions.

title: How Much Can We Expect to Spend in Retirement with a $4M Portfolio?
summary: Case study showing how a couple with $4 million portfolio can retire before age 65. Analyzes cash flow needs, withdrawal rates, and retirement timing flexibility. Demonstrates that achieving retirement targets does not require hitting a magic number but rather designing life goals and understanding income-expense dynamics to create financial intentionality.

title: I've Helped Hundreds of People Retire Comfortably. Here's the Exact 7-Step Framework I Used.
summary: James Conole presents the Sequoia System, a comprehensive 7-step retirement planning framework used by Root Financial Partners. The system guides retirees through defining their life vision, calculating retirement costs, projecting income sources, designing investment allocations, optimizing tax strategy, arranging insurance coverage, and establishing estate planning to create a sustainable retirement plan.

title: How Much Do You Need to Retire on $10K a Month?
summary: Framework for calculating retirement portfolio needs for $10K monthly spending. Examines three critical variables: income sources (Social Security, pensions), account types (Roth vs. traditional IRAs), and withdrawal rates, demonstrating how these factors reduce required portfolio size from $3.6M to as little as $1.6M depending on circumstances.

title: Stop Worrying About Running Out of Money: Here's What You Need to Do
summary: James Conole, CFP, addresses the fear of running out of money in retirement by citing research showing that a 4% withdrawal from a 60/40 portfolio has historically rarely depleted savings and often doubled or quintupled the portfolio over 30 years, then argues that being too conservative is an equally real risk, and outlines diversified investing and dynamic withdrawal guardrails as tools to enable higher sustainable spending.

title: 5 Things Retirees Regret - And What You Still Have Time to Get Right
summary: James Conole, CFP, describes five non-financial regrets he observes most often among retirees — failing to plan for life after work, neglecting health, letting relationships drift, expecting retirement to feel like a perpetual vacation, and being too afraid to spend — and argues these life-design issues matter more to retirement satisfaction than portfolio optimization.

title: Watch Me Stress Test a $1 Million Retirement Plan
summary: James Conole stress-tests a retirement plan for a couple (Jim and Sally, both 65) with just over $1 million in investments and a $2.1 million net worth including their home. The analysis shows their desired lifestyle requires a 13-14% withdrawal rate — unsustainable — and walks through trade-offs including working longer, cutting expenses, and downsizing to a less expensive home, which improves their probability of success from 13% to nearly 80%.

title: The Goal Isn’t Retirement. It’s Freedom to Live Fully.
summary: Manifesto advocating that true financial planning goal is not wealth accumulation but enabling freedom and fulfillment. Challenges the focus on portfolio balance growth in favor of aligning money with life's meaningful pursuits: purpose, adventure, relationships, health, and personal growth.

title: 5 Signs It’s Time to Retire - Right Now
summary: A financial adviser explores five non-financial reasons to retire now: time is non-renewable, prioritizing health before it deteriorates, maintaining relationships, maximizing healthspan rather than lifespan, and breaking free from work-identity dependence.

title: What Should You Do With Your 401k When You Retire?
summary: Examines the decision of whether to keep a 401(k) or roll it over to an IRA at retirement, covering six comparative factors (cost, control, investment options, consolidation, ease of use, and account coordination) and three advanced planning strategies: in-plan Roth conversions for after-tax contributions, Net Unrealized Appreciation for company stock, and the age-55 penalty-free withdrawal rule.

title: This Is Why Financially Ready People Struggle to Retire
summary: Explores why financially ready people struggle to retire by introducing the five types of wealth: financial, time, physical, social, and mental. Illustrates how overoptimizing for financial wealth can deplete other critical resources needed for a fulfilling retirement.

title: The #1 Way to Ruin Your Retirement (Most People Do This)
summary: Uses Shel Silverstein's poem 'Smart' to illustrate how many people ruin retirement by continuing to accumulate wealth after reaching financial independence, trading valuable time and health for money they no longer need. Emphasizes that financial plans should support meaningful living, not serve as ends themselves.

title: Can You Retire Without $1 Million? The Truth Might Surprise You
summary: James Conole, CFP, and co-host Erin challenge the widely cited million-dollar retirement benchmark, arguing that retirement readiness depends on cash flow—the gap between desired monthly expenses and guaranteed income sources—rather than on hitting a portfolio net worth target. Real-life examples of retirees who retired comfortably on $250,000–$300,000 illustrate how pensions, Social Security, and rental income can dramatically reduce the portfolio required.

title: Here's How Early Retirement Will Impact Your Social Security Benefit
summary: James Conole CFP and Erin (Erin Talks Money) clarify how early retirement affects Social Security benefits, explaining that the benefit is calculated from the 35 highest inflation-adjusted earning years rather than the age at which someone stops working, and distinguishing the work-cessation decision from the claiming decision.

title: When Money Isn’t Enough: The 5 Types of Wealth You Need Before Retiring
summary: Educational episode on five types of wealth beyond financial assets—time, social, mental, and physical wealth—and their importance in retirement planning. Discusses how focusing solely on financial optimization can erode other crucial forms of wealth, and offers practical strategies for balancing multiple dimensions of well-being during the transition to retirement.

title: They Gave Up the ‘Traditional’ Retirement to Live the Life They Always Wanted
summary: Interview with John and Bev, known as the Retirement Travelers, who retired at 55 and have since visited 107 countries and all 50 states. They discuss how a COVID-era Airstream trip revealed they could live with far less, leading them to sell their dream home and pursue full-time travel.

title: How to Avoid the Widow’s Tax (Before It’s Too Late)
summary: CFP James Conole explains the 'widow's tax' — tax bracket compression experienced by a surviving spouse — and presents three strategies to mitigate it: Roth conversions modeled under the single-survivor scenario, maximizing Social Security benefits to strengthen the income floor, and using the appropriate IRS life expectancy table (uniform, single life, or joint and last survivor) for required minimum distributions.

title: Retiring at 65 Might Be Too Late (Here’s Why)
summary: Examines the critical difference between lifespan and healthspan, showing how many retirees delay retirement to age 65 or later only to discover their healthiest years have passed. Argues that maximizing retirement savings while sacrificing health and relationships creates a poor quality-of-life tradeoff.

title: Root's State of the Union | What We’ve Done and Where We’re Headed
summary: Root Financial's annual State of the Union webinar hosted by founder James Conole and Chief Growth Officer Ari Taublieb covers the firm's growth from 22 to 55 team members and 450 to 725 clients over 12 months, a reduction in investment minimums to $1 million, the firm's mission-vision-values framework, team pod and squad structure, AI strategy focused on augmenting rather than replacing advisors, plans for a private wealth division, and ongoing challenges in mainta...

title: Top 5 Social Security Secrets Every 60-Year-Old Needs to Know
summary: Explores five critical Social Security strategies for retirees: understanding optimization goals (longevity insurance, spousal protection, legacy, investment returns), leveraging spousal and survivor benefits, managing tax liability (up to 85% taxable), coordinating work-while-claiming decisions, and separating retirement from benefit collection timing for tax-efficient income planning.

title: How Much Can I Spend In Retirement with a $2 Million Portfolio?
summary: James Conole, CFP, walks through a case study of Michael and Lisa, a couple in their early sixties with $2 million saved, whose initial retirement probability of success was 32%. Three targeted adjustments—separating time-limited travel spending from base expenses, shifting from a 60/40 to a growth allocation, and implementing systematic Roth conversions to the 12% bracket—raised their success probability to 74% without requiring additional savings or delayed retirement.

title: The Real Reason Most People Struggle in Retirement (It’s Not Money)
summary: Addresses the psychological, non-financial barriers to retirement satisfaction. Drawing on Morgan Housel's insights, the video argues that lacking a clear life vision leads retirees to mimic others' retirement blueprints, missing authentic fulfillment. A structured framework helps retirees define priorities (relationships, health, hobbies, faith, travel) and visualize daily life, before financial planning can effectively support true retirement goals.

title: What’s the Smartest Investment Mix for Retirees Right Now?
summary: James Conole, CFP, outlines a three-part framework for building a retirement investment portfolio that addresses volatility risk through a 'Root Reserves' bond/cash ladder, inflation risk through maintaining sufficient growth assets, and behavioral/emotional risk through appropriate fixed income allocation beyond pure financial need.

title: You Could Spend WAY More Than the 4% Rule Suggests (Says the Man Who Created It)
summary: Interview with Bill Bengen, creator of the 4% rule, discussing updated research showing retirees can safely withdraw 4.7% or higher from portfolios depending on diversification, inflation expectations, and market valuations. Covers the original 1994 research built on worst-case historical scenarios and how new asset classes and methodology increase sustainable withdrawal rates.

title: Roth vs. Traditional IRA – Which One Really Saves You More?
summary: Debunks three myths about Roth vs. traditional IRA accounts. The decision hinges on comparing current personal tax bracket with projected retirement tax bracket, not blanket rules. Demonstrates tax arbitrage opportunities through strategic Roth conversions during lower-income years. A case study shows how filling up tax brackets via conversion can generate $100,000+ in additional retirement assets through effective tax diversification and income control.

title: Advice from Retirees: What They Wish They Knew in Their 50s
summary: James Conole shares insights from his clients in their 70s and 80s about their biggest regrets and lessons learned, emphasizing the importance of treating wealth holistically (financial, time, and health), establishing a financial independence plan, prioritizing non-monetary values, and maintaining cognitive health through continuous learning and relationships.

title: How I Got to Throw the First Pitch for the Padres
summary: Personal narrative about throwing the first pitch for the San Diego Padres, connecting a childhood dream to adult life planning. Emphasizes that effective financial planning optimizes not just money but time, energy, and talents to enable memorable experiences and purposeful living. Argues that financial planning is meaningless unless aligned with intentional life experiences and relationships.

title: 5 Signs You Should Fire Your Financial Advisor
summary: Video outlines five critical red flags to watch for when evaluating your financial advisor relationship: over-reliance on CPAs for tax planning, investment-only focus, poor communication, neglecting spouses, and lack of genuine value-add. Emphasizes importance of holistic financial planning and clear communication.

title: The BEST Retirement Advice (From 909 Real Retirees)
summary: Based on survey feedback from 909 retirees, this video reveals the top retirement regrets and actionable insights: prioritize purpose over excessive savings, retire when ready rather than waiting for perfect conditions, design location thoughtfully, plan for tax spikes from RMDs and Social Security, maintain relationships, and don't delay spending in retirement.

title: The 3 Worst Retirement Mistakes I See All the Time
summary: Identifies three critical retirement mistakes: underestimating tax burdens (especially from RMDs and Social Security), underspending due to fear despite sufficient assets, and using outdated investment strategies optimized for accumulation rather than distribution. Proposes a three-bucket approach (growth, stable reserves, ballast) to manage sequence-of-returns risk while maintaining purchasing power.

title: $10M+ Net Worth Case Study: What to Do First
summary: James Conole and Ari Towi of Root Financial walk through a $14 million case study covering investment allocation, spending decisions, and sequence risk for a couple with inherited concentrated stock in Apple and Amazon, plus $4.5 million in retirement accounts. The video explores how portfolio mix and spending rates can swing outcomes from $70 million to insolvency at age 75, and previews upcoming content on tax and estate strategies.

title: 10 Ways Retirement Has Completely Changed in 30 Years
summary: Outlines 10 fundamental shifts reshaping modern retirement: psychological shift from scarcity to purpose-driven spending, increased life expectancy requiring longer-funded portfolios, decline of pensions shifting responsibility from employers to individuals, changed attitudes toward aging, rising healthcare costs, phased/flexible work transitions, lower bond yields reducing passive income, technology expanding lifestyle options, need for intentional community buildi...

title: This Retirement Math Might Change Your Timeline (Case Study)
summary: James Conole, CFP, walks through a case study of Michael and Lisa, a couple in their early 60s with roughly $2 million saved, to illustrate how front-loading travel spending, applying the retirement spending smile, and planning a mid-70s home downsize can achieve the same probability of retirement success as working three extra years — while preserving prime healthy years.

title: 3 Investments That Should Never Go In Your Retirement Portfolio
summary: Examines why gold, concentrated stock positions, and home equity should be excluded from retirement portfolios. Gold's historical returns lag stocks with higher volatility and prolonged downturns; concentrated positions increase portfolio volatility without enhancing returns; and primary residences generate no cash flow. Effective retirement planning requires growth assets to outpace inflation and reserve assets to cushion market downturns.

title: Your Tax Planning Window Won’t Last Forever | Top 3 Strategies to Prioritize
summary: James Conole and Ari Towel of Root Financial explain the three critical tax strategies for early retirees: Roth conversions (converting pre-tax accounts at low tax brackets), ACA health insurance subsidies (managing income below thresholds), and 0% long-term capital gains harvesting. Demonstrates through case study that optimal prioritization depends on account mix and spending levels, not one-size-fits-all rules.

title: Retirement Income Strategy Explained — How to Pay Yourself for Life
summary: A comprehensive framework for converting retirement savings into a sustainable income stream using three steps: determining a safe withdrawal rate (4-5%), optimizing tax efficiency through strategic income source coordination, and coordinating portfolio withdrawals with Social Security and other income sources. Demonstrates how careful tax planning in retirement can reduce effective tax rates significantly compared to working years.

title: The Unexpected Exit at 55 — Finding Freedom, Purpose, and Identity | Retirement Reality
summary: Interview with retiree Donovan Bulk, hosted by CFP James Conole, covering Donovan's unexpected exit from corporate life at 55, the psychological and identity challenges of early retirement, the importance of financial preparation and compounding, and practical frameworks including four phases of retirement and a happiness curve model.

title: Retirement Benchmarks by Age: 40, 50, 60 (And What If You’re Behind?)
summary: Critiques age-based retirement benchmarks (3x salary by 40, 6x by 50, 10x by 67) and presents a personalized four-step framework: estimate retirement expenses, calculate non-portfolio income (Social Security, pensions), determine the gap to fund from investments, and apply withdrawal rates to size the required portfolio. Real examples show how timing, external income sources, and windfall expectations dramatically alter adequacy.

title: How to Maximize Social Security Spousal Benefits (Even if You Collect Your Benefit Early)
summary: Explains how Social Security spousal benefits work, covering eligibility rules, the cap at 50% of the primary earner's full retirement age benefit, how early or delayed filing by the primary earner affects spousal benefit amounts, survivor benefits, and how the spousal benefit combines one's own earnings record with excess spousal benefit.

title: Retiring at 54: Why Darren Says It Feels Surreal | Retirement Reality
summary: James Conole interviews Darren, who retired at age 54 after 30 years of work, eight months prior to recording. The conversation covers how Darren hit his financial target and paid off his mortgage, waited four years before a company reorg provided the right moment to leave, and how early retirement has felt surreal but manageable.

title: The Secret Cost of Claiming Social Security Too Early (or Too Late)
summary: Examines Social Security claiming timing as a multi-dimensional retirement strategy decision that affects taxes, portfolio growth, spousal survivor benefits, and Roth conversion windows — not just a break-even calculation. Presents pros and cons of claiming at 62, full retirement age (67), and 70, illustrated with a dynamic strategy that adjusts to market conditions.

title: Why He Walked Away From Work at 57 — and Never Looked Back | Retirement Reality
summary: James Conole interviews Michael, who retired at 57 after his wife's cancer diagnosis reframed his relationship with time and work. Michael describes his early financial habits — investing heavily in a 401k and dividend reinvestment plans from the start of his career — as the foundation that made early retirement possible.

title: Retiring With a Pension? Here’s How It Changes Everything About Your Retirement Math
summary: Explains how pensions fundamentally change retirement planning by shifting focus from net worth to cash flow. A $60,000 annual pension is equivalent to a $1.5 million portfolio at 4% withdrawal rates. Case studies show couples with strong pensions may need $600K-$750K portfolios (versus $1.25M without pensions), and those with pensions fully covering expenses may need no portfolio withdrawals.

title: Early Retirement at 51: How He Turned an Unexpected Ending into a New Beginning | Retirement Reality
summary: Interview with James Menddees, a 51-year-old tech worker who was laid off unexpectedly after planning to retire at 55, exploring how he navigated the financial and psychological transition from forced early retirement. He discusses his decades-long savings strategy, working with a financial advisor, the challenges of identity loss post-career, and ultimately embracing the layoff as a catalyst for freedom.

title: Why Delaying Social Security Destroys Your Portfolio
summary: Challenges the universal advice to delay Social Security until age 70. While delaying does maximize monthly benefits (8% annual increase), it creates hidden costs: portfolio drawdowns during the delay period, longevity risk if life expectancy is moderate, and reduced flexibility.

title: Retired at 50 Freedom, Guilt… and What No One Tells You | Retirement Reality
summary: Interview with Kent, a 50-year-old retiree nine months into early retirement, conducted by CFP James Conole. Kent describes the financial path, inherited wealth and associated guilt, emotional challenges of telling family and coworkers, and the psychological identity shift required when leaving work two decades early.

title: Why Singles Are Retiring 5 Years Too Late
summary: Financial advisor James Conole presents a three-step retirement planning framework for singles: defining a freedom number, mapping reliable income sources (Social Security/pensions), and building a portfolio with purpose. Using 62-year-old Tina with $2.2M in assets as a case study, he demonstrates how singles can retire earlier and spend more while maintaining a sustainable withdrawal rate.

title: Freedom at 60 Why He Left a Job He Loved (and Doesn’t Regret It) | Retirement Reality
summary: Steve, a retired video game programmer, shares his journey to early retirement at 60 with financial planner James Conole. A 2021 heart attack served as the primary catalyst, prompting Steve to formalize retirement plans, adopt budgeting (YNAB), maximize 401k and HSA contributions, and plan Roth conversions. He discusses life after work including jazz bands, acting classes, VR-based fitness, and travel.

title: Stop Doing Roth Conversions Until You See This
summary: James Conole explains how Roth conversions can trigger unintended Medicare IRMAA surcharges costing thousands annually. The key insight is that IRMAA charges are based on modified adjusted gross income (MAGI), not taxable income. A case study shows a couple could save nearly $1M in taxes but lose tens of thousands to Medicare surcharges by converting to the wrong bracket.

title: The Hardest Year of Retirement: What No One Warned Me About | Retirement Reality
summary: A Retirement Reality interview with Jim, a former software engineer who retired after 36 years, covering the psychological challenges of retirement including identity loss, the difficulty of finding purpose, and how volunteering and writing ultimately provided fulfillment in year four.

title: Here's the Most Unethical Thing Advisors Do
summary: Financial advisor James Conole critiques unethical advisor practices that prioritize demonstrating fee value through impressive tax savings over understanding clients' actual life goals. Using Jim and Sally's case, he shows how an advisor can claim nearly $900,000 in tax value while ignoring lifestyle priorities, but that same strategy yields only $400,000 when spending reflects their actual desired life.

title: 18 Months Into Retirement, This is What Surprised Me Most | Retirement Reality
summary: James Conole interviews Christian, a retiree 18 months into retirement after 30+ years in a global chemical company, about the emotional and financial realities of leaving work.

title: Retiring After 65? The Rules Change (Hint: You Can Spend More)
summary: James Conole, CFP, outlines how retirement planning changes when someone retires after age 65, covering adjusted withdrawal rates, optimizing limited go-go years, market downturn risk with compressed recovery time, compressed Roth conversion windows before RMDs, IRMAA surcharges, survivor planning, and the importance of health investment.

title: This Is What a $10M Retirement Actually Looks Like
summary: James Conole, CFP, uses a detailed case study of a couple with approximately $10 million in assets to illustrate what a high-net-worth retirement actually looks like, covering cash flow modeling, withdrawal rates, tax planning, RMDs, estate planning, and the psychological challenge of transitioning to intentional spending.

title: I’ve Never Seen So Many Retirees Make This Same Mistake
summary: James Conole identifies two common retirement portfolio mistakes — maintaining an overly concentrated growth portfolio from accumulation years and accepting a generic 60/40 allocation from an advisor — and explains through case studies how both fail to address individual cash-flow needs. He argues that portfolios must be designed from the inside out, starting with a financial plan rather than a default asset allocation.

title: You Can Retire… But You Might Not Like the Life You Built
summary: James Conole addresses why many retirees with strong finances (no debt, pension, Social Security, $2M+ portfolios) remain unhappy. Financial planning software answers whether you can retire but not what you're retiring into. He identifies three hidden risks: identity shock from losing work identity, unstructured time becoming emotionally heavy despite freedom, and spouse relationship friction from 24/7 togetherness.

title: You Need to Retire Early - Here's Why
summary: James Conole, CFP, uses a case study of a 55-year-old couple with $2.3 million to argue that everyone should plan for early retirement as a mindset rather than a destination. He demonstrates through cash-flow modeling and Monte Carlo analysis that working until 65 can result in dying with excess wealth, while retiring at 60 or 62 captures irreplaceable years of health and vitality.

title: Work at SpaceX? Watch This Before your IPO
summary: Financial planner James Conole presents a framework for SpaceX employees approaching a potential 2026 IPO, arguing that the first priority is defining desired life outcomes before deciding how much equity to sell, hold, or diversify.

title: Why $5 Million is the Tax "Danger Zone"
summary: James Conole explains why portfolios above $5 million require a lifetime tax minimization strategy rather than annual tax reduction, covering strategic withdrawals across account types, intentional Roth conversions timed to tax-planning windows, asset location optimization, and capital gains and charitable planning strategies including donor-advised funds and qualified charitable distributions.

title: The Biggest Retirement Lie: "I Can’t Retire Until Medicare"
summary: James Conole challenges the assumption that Medicare is a prerequisite for retirement, presenting a case study of a couple in their late 50s who can retire early with affordable health insurance alternatives (COBRA, ACA marketplace, private plans) rather than waiting until age 65. The video demonstrates that treating health insurance as an expense rather than a gatekeeper enables meaningful early retirement.

title: The Retirement Red Zone: Why the Final 5 Years Decide Everything
summary: James Conole, CFP, outlines five principles for the final five years before retirement: the portfolio tipping point where growth exceeds contributions, building an income plan rather than targeting a portfolio number, understanding sequence of returns risk, treating taxes as the single largest retirement expense, and designing a fulfilling life plan rather than just a financial spreadsheet. He argues that small decisions in this window can outweigh the prior twenty years of saving.

title: 4 Retirement Income Strategies: Which one Wins in 2026?
summary: Compares four retirement income strategies — single premium immediate annuities, dividend-only investing, the 4% rule, and a guardrails-based dynamic withdrawal approach — using a $1 million hypothetical portfolio, outlining the tradeoffs of each and arguing that the guardrails framework best balances protection with upside spending flexibility.

title: The Dark Truth About Retirement (No One Tells You This)
summary: James Conole discusses the psychological and emotional phases of retirement beyond financial planning, presenting research-backed insights on how retirees experience a honeymoon phase followed by disillusionment and identity loss. He advocates shifting focus from lifespan to healthspan, avoiding three common retirement traps, and creating intentional daily rhythms around relationships, health, and purpose.

title: "Only Live Off Dividends" Is Your Biggest Portfolio Risk in Retirement
summary: James Conole, CFP of Root Financial, debunks the popular retirement strategy of living only off dividends and never touching principal, showing that chasing high dividend yields concentrates portfolios in narrow sectors, exposes retirees to greater sequence of returns risk, and reduces long-term total return. He proposes a total return framework with a reserve buffer instead.

title: No Amount of Money Will Give you Permission to Retire - But This Will
summary: James Conole reframes retirement preparation from waiting until a specific age to 'firing your employer'—a mindset shift that means replacing employer-dependent income, health insurance, structure, identity, and purpose well before retirement. He distinguishes logistical dependencies (paycheck, health insurance) from psychological ones (identity, purpose, structure and relationships), arguing that true retirement readiness requires addressing both.

title: Stop Working for Money You’ll Never Spend
summary: James Conole argues that retirees should fear running out of time more than running out of money. Using Michael Kitces' research on the 4% rule, he demonstrates that most retirees end retirement with nearly 3x their starting portfolio balance, yet continue working due to mindset issues (difficulty transitioning from saving to spending) rather than financial necessity.

title: Why You Still Can’t Retire (Even If You Have Enough)
summary: Explores five mental traps that prevent people from retiring despite having sufficient financial resources: questioning readiness, perpetual postponement, feeling like savings are never enough, loss of work identity, and fear of uncertainty. Argues that financial security comes from strategy rather than specific portfolio amounts.

title: Stop Overfunding Your 401(k). Do This Instead
summary: Argues that excessive 401(k) contributions can create tax-inefficient, illiquid retirement portfolios despite large balances. Advocates for diversified account structures combining pre-tax, Roth, and brokerage accounts to maximize flexibility, control tax timing, and reduce psychological barriers to spending in retirement.

title: You Don’t Need a Financial Advisor… Until You Do (Here’s When)
summary: Educational video on when you need a financial advisor. Addresses misconceptions that advisors are only for fixing broken situations or beating the market. Explores five key areas where advisors add value: portfolio strategy during retirement, tax optimization, hidden costs of DIY approaches, surviving spouse insurance, and behavioral guardrails during market downturns.

title: You’re Thinking About Social Security Wrong… It’s Not About Not 62 vs. 67 vs. 70
summary: James Conole, CFP, reframes the Social Security claiming decision as a risk management problem rather than a break-even math exercise, analyzing what collecting at 62, 67, or 70 protects against and what new risks each choice introduces. Key topics include the earnings limit, survivor benefit implications for married couples, the interaction between delayed claiming and portfolio sequence-of-return risk, and the tax planning window available when Social Security is deferred.

title: $15M in Nvidia Stock Case Study | Don't Just "Diversify Everything"
summary: Case study analyzing how a family with $15M in concentrated Nvidia stock can strategically diversify to achieve financial security without incurring massive tax liabilities. Presents practical strategies including separately managed accounts, tax-loss harvesting, mega backdoor Roths, and portfolio-level coordination to balance growth with peace of mind.

title: Retire at 60? Watch This Before You Claim Social Security
summary: Explains how retiring at 60 affects Social Security benefits through gaps in the 35-year earnings history calculation, covers spousal and survivor benefit mechanics, discusses early claiming penalties and delayed retirement credits, and addresses the earnings limit for those who collect before full retirement age.

title: Taxes on a $3M Retirement Portfolio (What You'll Actually Owe Each Year)
summary: James Conole, CFP, compares two $3M retirement portfolios—one all-traditional IRA and one diversified across IRA, Roth, and brokerage—to illustrate how account type, not portfolio size, drives long-term tax liability through RMDs, Medicare IRMAA surcharges, and bracket compression at death of a spouse.

title: Why I Told My Client Not to Pay Off Their Mortgage Before Retiring
summary: Challenges the conventional wisdom of paying off mortgages before retirement through a case study of a client who delayed retirement five years to eliminate an $1,800 monthly payment. Argues that focus should be on cash flow sustainability rather than debt elimination, and warns about sequence-of-returns risk when carrying fixed debt into retirement.

title: The Real Math of Working 1 More Year (It’s Not What You Think)
summary: Examines the hidden cost of working one additional year before retirement through a case study of Mark and Carol, who discovered that an extra $600-700 monthly spending power didn't align with their actual retirement priorities. Emphasizes that the true calculation should balance financial gain against loss of healthspan and quality years, not just portfolio growth.

title: If You Only Watch One Retirement Video, Make it This
summary: James Conole, CFP and CEO of Root Financial, presents seven laws of retirement planning covering the psychological, temporal, and financial dimensions of the retirement transition.

title: 7 Investments That Will REDUCE Your Taxes Immediately
summary: Dave Zoller walks through seven investments that reduce taxes. Four common ones (oil and gas partnerships, real estate, municipal bonds, traditional IRA/401k) and three less-discussed (HSA, small business, donor-advised fund / impact investing). For each, he scores tax deduction on contribution, tax on income, and tax on sale. Recommends impact investing via a DAF as the best-deduction option since the full contribution is deductible immediately.

title: I Retired With Millions BUT Made 6 BIG Mistakes
summary: Dave Zoller walks through six post-sale mistakes business owner client 'Dan' made transitioning into retirement: (1) underestimating loss of control, (2) lifestyle-expense changes when business deductions vanish, (3) jumping into 'attractive' deals too fast, (4) missing pre-sale tax strategies like charitable pre-funding, (5) skipping asset-protection planning, (6) handing wealth to unprepared heirs. Opens with Arthur Brooks' fluid-vs-crystallized intelligence frame.

title: They Retired 10 Years Early Due To 3 Things
summary: Dave Zoller walks through a real-client case: Mary and John, 58 with $2M saved, told by a CFP they had to work 12 more years before retiring. Reframes the planning conversation around life values FIRST (faith, family, fitness, fun, friends) rather than numbers-first. After exploring what-if scenarios (move to South Carolina to be near grandkids, drop $25K/yr country club, reduce travel spend), the plan shifted from 12 more years to ~2 — same risk tolerance, totally different outcome.

title: Leadership, Transitions and Legacy Impact w/ Bill Holbrook
summary: Long-form Wealth Beyond Riches podcast interview with Bill Holbrook, executive coach to Streamline Financial. Covers Bill's path from youth pastor to executive coach, how he uses StrengthsFinder/Myers-Briggs/Enneagram as dynamic assessments rather than boxes, identity loss in retirement (the 'RW word'), the importance of leaving a leadership legacy via succession planning (2-4 year timeline), defining 'enough' to resist scaling, and modeling rhythms of rest (daily/weekly/sabbatical vacations).

title: 4 Uncomfortable Retirement Truths You Need To Hear
summary: Dave Zoller shares four uncomfortable retirement truths that distinguish fulfilled retirees from those who struggle: (1) financial success doesn't guarantee retirement success — identity, purpose, and health matter as much; (2) family caregiving for elderly parents is a common, often overlooked retirement-time cost; (3) retirement isn't a stress-free vacation — passive-income stress and news anxiety emerge; (4) the saver-to-spender transition is harder than expected even with abundant savings.

title: 4 Reasons To Do ROTH Conversions If You Have A Pension
summary: Dave Zoller argues retirees with pensions have a stronger case for Roth conversions than those without. Three reasons: (1) pensions plus Social Security plus RMDs at 73 can push retirees into higher brackets they don't need the cash for; (2) higher taxable income drives Medicare IRMAA surcharges; (3) the surviving spouse's tax filing status (single vs married) compounds the bracket impact. Recommends using the pre-RMD/pre-Social-Security window as the Roth conversion 'sweet spot.'

title: 9 Things to STOP DOING After 55 To Enjoy Life More
summary: Dave Zoller lists nine things the happiest retirees they've worked with STOPPED doing after 55 to increase retirement satisfaction. The list: stop taking time for granted (life-in-weeks visualization), stop saving unnecessarily, stop compromising health, stop worrying so much, stop spending time with draining people, stop spending on things not aligned with values, stop over-supporting adult children, stop pretending to be someone you're not, stop living with regret.

title: He Never Expected THIS Problem In Retirement
summary: Dave Zoller shares a real client (early-50s retiree, one year in) who faced unexpected social pressure: family and acquaintances kept saying he was too young, asking what he'd do all day. Reframes the answer with three practices — rest, test, learn — plus play. Highlights investing not just money but also TIME in three areas often missed by financial advice: curiosities (hobbies/passions), relationships, and contribution to others.

title: The Journey of Reinvention: Mikey Taylor's Path from Pro Skateboarding to Real Estate
summary: Wealth Beyond Riches interview with Mikey Taylor — former pro skateboarder, Saint Archer co-founder (sold to MillerCoors), now real-estate fund manager at Commune Capital. Covers his identity collapse after losing his skate sponsor, year-long Bible reading reset, what financial freedom means, building parallel brand equity, real-estate macro (multifamily, storage, hospitality), and his next chapter on marriage retreats.

title: Vanguard Predicts It Won't Be As Good Moving Forward
summary: Dave Zoller breaks down Vanguard's decade outlook (~2024-2034): US bonds projected to return 4.8-5.8% nominal annualized (much higher than pre-rate-hikes); international bonds 4.7-5.7%. 60/40 portfolio's probability of hitting 7%+ annualized increased. Vanguard sees US equities (especially large-cap growth) overvalued, with emerging markets and small-cap potentially undervalued. Cautions that Vanguard's 2014 decade forecast called for 5-8% global equity returns — actual was ~12%/yr.

title: Common Withdrawal Mistakes Retirees Make
summary: Dave Zoller covers three common retirement-withdrawal mistakes: (1) following the default 'taxable first, then tax-deferred, then Roth' order without modeling — can lead to forced early Social Security and inflated RMDs; (2) not mapping fluctuating spending across go-go vs slow-go years; (3) misaligning investments with the income plan, so a sequence-of-returns shock forces selling at a loss. Cites the Vanguard Advisor Alpha study claiming withdrawal-order choice is worth up to 1.2%/yr.

title: When To Start Collecting Social Security Benefits
summary: Dave Zoller walks through a visual decision-tree for when to start Social Security (62, full retirement age, or 70), using a hypothetical 63-year-old retired example. Shows the key flaw — the diagram ignores spousal/survivor-benefit dynamics. Then introduces a JPMorgan longevity-probability chart and a return-vs-longevity grid: every year deferred past full retirement age boosts benefits ~8%, so compare against your expected portfolio return.

title: Don't Let This Happen In Retirement
summary: Dave Zoller argues that the 'stress' retirees lose at work isn't all bad — beneficial eustress (challenge, progress toward goals) disappears too, and its absence is linked to faster mental/physical decline. Issues a 30-for-30 challenge: pick an area of growth, commit 30 minutes a day for 30 consecutive days, track on a paper calendar, and tell someone for accountability.

title: 2025 Social Security Increase
summary: Dave Zoller delivers the 2025 Social Security COLA estimate (~2.6%, down from 3.2% in 2024 and 8.7% in 2023) and walks through three core retirement-income risks: longevity (running out of money), inflation, and exposure to bad markets. Notes Social Security helps with all three; pensions and certain annuities address one or two. Recap of the 1982 Social Security fix as evidence Congress will likely paper over the 2033 trust-fund shortfall.

title: Unlocking Integrity and Generosity: A Journey with John Blumberg
summary: Wealth Beyond Riches interview with John Blumberg — former 18-year Arthur Andersen partner turned leadership coach and author of Return on Integrity. Covers leaving Andersen five years before its Enron collapse, redefining integrity as wholeness, the difference between estate planning and wealth transfer, why defining 'when is enough enough' prevents wealth from gripping you, and the importance of 'drift catchers' who tell you the truth.

title: The Month You Retire Really Matters
summary: Dave Zoller breaks down how the month you retire can affect your finances and well-being. Covers three categories: tax benefits (retiring in the first half of the year opens Roth-conversion, tax-gain-harvesting, and 401(k)-max-out options), non-financial benefits (spring/summer retirement avoids Northern-state winter depression), and financial considerations (pension full-eligibility dates, Medicare/COBRA timing, year-end bonuses, accrued PTO).

title: RETIREMENT EXPERT: They'll Be Caught Off Guard When This Happens In Retirement - Ric Edelman
summary: Dave Zoller interviews Ric Edelman (founder of Edelman Financial Engines, ~$250B AUM) on the future of retirement. Edelman argues longevity (likely 95-100 for those alive in 2030) makes the traditional 60s retirement and 60/40 portfolio obsolete. Recommends 70/30 or 75/25 well into the 80s, ~20% allocation to exponential technologies (AI, robotics, biotech, blockchain/crypto, including 1-3% in Bitcoin), and reinventing for the next 30-40-year chapter via new.

title: Why Most Retirees Secretly Struggle
summary: Dave Zoller covers the four most common reasons retirees secretly struggle: (1) time-alone discomfort (cites the University of Virginia electric-shock-button study where 67% of men shocked themselves rather than sit alone); (2) the wealth-health-time triangle imbalance — the equilateral-triangle phase in the 50s-60s is the magical retirement window; (3) the spending paradox (Kitces study: 2/3 of 4% retirees end with more than 2x their starting principal); (4).

title: The Intersection of Wellness and Leadership with Jackie Glass
summary: Tim hosts executive wellness coach Jackie Glass on Wealth Beyond Riches. They explore why leaders are loneliest during transitions (not at the top), introduce Carey Lohrenz's prepare/perform/prevail framework for life change, and discuss the practice of 'margin' and the principle of working from a place of rest rather than resting from a place of work. Closes with stories from the Global Leadership Summit, including hosting Joni Eareckson Tada.

title: One BAD Decision Ruined His Retirement
summary: Dave Zoller tells the story of two retirees who started March 2020 with similar plans: Jane and 'Kevin.' Both panicked during the 30%+ drop, but Jane focused on the seven-year safe-money bucket and held; Kevin sold to cash and never got back in. Jane then layered three winning moves — tax-loss harvesting, a Roth conversion at depressed values, and a system-driven rebalance from bonds into stocks.
